content: The Antioch arrives on the outskirts of the Obroai Skai System, to see a small flotilla of 4 ISDs (Chimaera, Death's Head, Judicator, Inexorable), at the center of which, floats the Grand Admiral's Flagship, underpainted with the Dragons of Chilla.

Thrawn's alien face pops up on the central holodisplay: **"Lt. Ardena. I received your hypercom request for Rendezvous. You say you've urgent news to impart regarding the location of Mount Tantiss, but that it wouldn't be safe to transmit via holonet. I am very eager to hear its location now that we're on a closed direct channel. I want those cloning cylinders in my possession, as soon as Chissably possible."**
Lexi takes a breath as the image of Thrawn comes over the holodisplay. "**Admiral,**" she says in greeting. "**We've learned Mt Tantis is on the planet Wayland.**"
Thrawn narrows his eyes, **"How accurate is this information, and what is its source."**
"**The head scientist of the Reaper project, Xod'mon'orkhez. He passed the knowledge to us before his death.**"

** “Excellent, I believe you’re right. All of my intelligence sources also point to Wayland as a high potentiality. The past few hours, I’ve already taken the liberty of preparing Battle Group 1 for an assault on Mount Tantiss. All I needed from you, Lt., was the final location of the facility. I am going to insist, that your ship be detained in my Docking Bay, in case you’re sending me into a trap. Our tractor beams are already locked onto you. Once we arrive at Wayland, we should be able to scan for mountains that have been artificially civilized. By all accounts, Mount Tantiss is a major complex that is supposedly hard to miss, as it was basically built upon an abandoned planet the facility should stand out like a sore thumb.”**
**"Engines Report: Tractor beams have overriden my trajectory, Captain."**
**"There's one more small matter of business... Commander Breggor Kryze failed to report in. I see he is not present on the bridge. Care to explain, Lt.?"**

Lexi nods to NZ and then turns back to Thrawn.. "**There is one complication, Admiral. We won't be the first ones to be... Engaging the facility. There's a third party ahead of us.**" When he asks about Breggor, something she knew he would be asking about she pauses only a moment. "**I'm sorry to report Commander Kryze has fallen in the pursuit of his duty. Just two days ago our ship was infiltrated by a Reaper and he died in our defense.**"

**"I understand. He shall be properly mourned in due course of time. However, presently, time is of the essence, if indeed a rival of mine is planning to seize the facility ahead of my flotilla. Tell me everything you know about this New Complication."**

Lexi takes a breath and glances over at Wraith for a moment before explaining to Thrawn about Charybdis program and how she and Wraith suspected the infected Reaper may be after the hexalite or knowledge of the hexalite.

**"So... To summarize, an Infected Reaper is going to the Storehouse ahead of us presumably to procure this rare substance, Hexalite. I'm not even sure how to respond to that. In either case, a single Reaper cannot possibly outgun our flotilla, if he is piloting a single outdated shuttle. I will be sure to submit this description to our Weapons Officer, for immediate termination upon sight, but otherwise there is nothing else I can do to mitigate this individual's mission at this time."**

**"I am preparing to jump to hyperspace immediately by the shortest route to Wayland. Do you have any additional information to report now, that would be pertinent to our imminent siege of the Emperor's Storehouse?"**

Lexi takes a moment to consider before answering. "**No, sir. This Reaper's arrival before us may put the base on alert. From our earlier calculations he could have at least 12 hours there before our arrival.**"

**"I see, that could prove to be a problem, if a local Imperial Sector Fleet reponds to a high priority request for reinforcements, then we could be in for quite a fight. I've taken the liberty of repainting our Star Destroyers to clearly demarcate our fleet, so keep in mind that any grey-colored ships should be assumed as enemies."**
**"I don't expect that a single Reaper can call forth such reinforcement, however, and I anticipate, at least initially, that our Orbital Operation should proceed with relative alacrity."**

"**This is not mearly a Reaper. The modification he's undergone has improved his healing to the point he can take a remarkable amount of injuries in a short frame of time. If there is a stockpile of the hexalite, he could create more homunculi and become nearly invincible himself.**"

Thrawn smiles cooly, **"I have yet to meet a Reaper invincible in the face of a full barrage of turbolasers. I shall take your warnings under advisement. Moving on.. I have a proposition for you. As you recall, our deal clearly involved that you, Force Users both, would help to clear the facility of enemy Reapers in conjunction with my seizing it. I don't expect you to just go and die for a lost cause, but instead I wish to offer you both as much support as I can, in the Ground Assault Portion of the Siege of Wayland. My mighty warships will not be able to give you full aerial support whilst the Planetary Shield remains operational. Therefore... I have prepared a Strike Force of AT-AT Walkers, and several smaller supporting ground units, which I wish you to take command of personally. More information will be forthcoming during hyperspace transit. My officers are informing me that we are nearly ready for hyperjump. We shall resume this conversation, in route to our Objective. Thrawn out."**

The Antioch shudders as it is swallowed by the Chimaera’s docking bay, entering the environmental shield and landing amidst a clearly high alert area. The TIE squadrons have all been set to maximum readiness, pilots not quite standing by, but certainly fuel readings all topped off and munitions loaded. 

Once aboard, not two minutes pass, before the view outside changes to that of swirling blue hyperspace lights alongside a lurching sensation. The crew of the Antioch is nearly bowled off their feet, but manage to keep their balance not to bust open their heads. It seems the Grand Admiral was in no mood to delay, given the news about the New Complication.

While in transit, Lexi able to establish synchronization with the Chimaera’s holonet. She is relieved to see that her crew’s ranks have been untouched, but instead, that Thrawn has marked both Lexi and Kai for promotions. Lexi has been promoted to Commodore, while Wraith has apparently advanced to Captain himself. Sgt. Kryze has been posthumously promoted to Master Sgt.

Lexi listens to Thrawn's plan and is glad she and Wraith would have support as they engaged the forces at the base. Signing off from the transmission with Thrawn she feels the sickening pull of the transition to hyperspace in addition to being nearly knocked out of her seat from the suddenness of the transition. For a moment she unknowingly looks a bit pale, before her skin flushes with color once more as her attention turns to the updated feed. 

"**Well... I suppose it's going as well as we could expect. Thrawn has promoted us,**" she says, looking back at Wraith. "**Congratulations... Captain,**" she smirks at him.

**"Likewise, congratulations are in order for you as well, Commodore Ardena. If ranks matter, and they aren't just some fancy words attached to your name that make you feel all goody-goody inside your tummy while you go out onto the battlefield to catch live fire rounds."**
He leans back in the copilot's seat, eyes narrowing pensively, **"Which sounds like what we'll be doing when we arrive on Wayland, by the way."**

"**I always suspected he'd have us lead the vangaurd on Mt Tantis... he wanted us to keep the Reapers busy while he secures the cloning facility. At least he's providing us with a strike force, which is a lot more than I initially thought.**"

**"Right, but there's also the opponents we'll be facing. Presumably a small hidden research facility, wouldn't have obvious army bases nearby to protect it, but it could have some formidable defenses of its own. No doubt its rigged to blow, or at least collapse, if overtaken by enemy forces. I don't want to be anywhere near that when or if it happens. To fulfil your Prophecy, all we need do would be to destroy the Emperor Reborn before he can travel back in time 5 years to the Battle of Endor. I, of course, intend to slay the Reaper Queen and the rest of her Broodlings. Thrawn's Objective is to seize the Cloning Cylinders, as well as any other secret military technology, prototypes, and resources for his Organization. The Dark One, presumably, is after the Hexalite stores that are likely already there. The way I see it, our best course of action would be to trigger the self-destruct. That would take care of all our enemies at the same time, although it would inconvenience Thrawn. However, I think if we obtain for Thrawn the schematics for the Cloning Cylinders, and a couple actual cloning cylinder prototypes, as well as a data dump of the information stored at Mount Tantiss' databanks... Thrawn might be mollified, and count that as a partial victory. The sole problem being... we'd have to be present to make absolute Sure the Emperor Reborn is dead first, before we blow up the whole Mountain. That would necessitate direct infiltration of the reserach facility... Where I was 'born'..."** He trails off in thought.

Lexi waits for a moment to see if he's going to finish his thought. "**Ultimately, we're not in an ideal situation. In order to deal with the clone and have enough to mullify Thrawn we will need to infilatrate into the heart of the facility.**" She pauses "**All while dealing with the Dark One and any forces stationed here.**"

**"Well, the way I see it, we might actually be at an advantage thanks to my being a Reaper. While we let Thrawn's AT-AT combined ground forces assault the Mountain, our enemies will be distracted and become embroiled in the military conflict ensuing from that. All eyes will be the front lines, whilst all we have to do is sneak into the Mountain from a different direction entirely, around the same time, and make use of the distraction to infiltrate deep enough to uncover the Sleeping Emporer Reborn, purge his cloning tank, load a few tanks onto a hoversled, and high tail it outa there before anyone is none the wiser. Plus, if we're ever stopped by the guards, we can say we belong there, on account of us being Reapers who were awakened to deal with the ground invasion."**

**"Once we're out of the Mountain, we just signal Thrawn to drop the hammer on the facility with Orbital Bombardment, although he might be too greedy to destroy so many cloning cylinders. So... ideally, we should also find a way to trigger a self-destruct, or at the very least, blow up a couple fuel depots on the way out that would trigger an explosion cascade large enough to bring the facility down for good."**

Lexi listens to Wraith's plan. It was perhaps optimistic, but without more information was going to be the better option. "**We should take NZ with us to aid us in retrieving as much as we can from the databanks.**" She glances over at the droid. "**What will you need for the data retrieval, NZ?**"
**"Just my builtin scomplink, Commodore, as well as time to crack the high-security codes that are certain to be guarding the information stores within a top-secret Imperial Research Base."**

**"Calculating Probabilities: I'm not 100% certain I can slice through at all, however, having Imperial Code Cylinders belonging to a high ranking base official, would no doubt speed up my progress. Without them, I calculate only 13% probability of being able to breech the in-built top secret firewall protections."**
**"I will be vulnerable while I'm slicing their security firewalls, so I will require defense during this part of the operation."**

"**There may be opportunity to retrieve a code cylinder. I'm sure there's still a number of scientists and staff to maintain the equipment and awaken new Reapers as needed.**" With a nod she tries to reassure the droid. "**We'll watch your back so you can do your job.**" 
"**Now it's just a matter of determining what resources Thrawn is providing and how we can use them to create a distraction to gain access to the base... and where that access will be.**"

**"Sounds like our checklist to get out of there, just grew a bit."** He starts ticking off his fingers one at a time: **"1. Infiltrate the base, whilst simultaneously triggering a ground assault. 2. Collect top-secret Code Cylinders from a high ranking base official. 3. Kill the Emperor Reborn, 4. Kill the Reaper Queen. 5. Kill the Dark One. 6. Slice the database for goodies to give away to Thrawn. 7. Collect Cloning Cylinders for Thrawn. 8. Leave and set off explosive charges on the way out, hopefully killing anyone above that we couldn't find. 9. Watch the timeline repair itself, as the Prophecy is fulfilled. 10. Profit.  That about cover it?"**
**"First things first, I'm going to request 3 more Speeder Bikes to replace those we... uh, lost... in the Supervolcano explosion."** He hits a few keys on the console.

Lexi cocks her head as she considers one of his points. "**Do you really think the timeline will just... repair itself?**" It was something she'd been thinking about since those first days on Mykyr. "**If that was the case... then we'd only have to worry about killing the clone and everything else would be unnecessary...**" Her gaze goes to Wraith.
If time repaired itself, she'd never have left Tython and Wraith would likely never had been 'born'.

Looking up sheepishly, he shrugs, **"In theory, yes, but the problem is we don't know what would have happened if the clone had never gone back in time to Endor. Also, we don't know that there isn't a second Mount Tantiss with a second clone, ready to step in if the first one is killed, which is what our plan is to do."**
**"To make matters more complex, some theories of time travel state that every time travel point creates a new timeline, thus splitting off reality into multiple realities."**

**"Our Reborn Universe may be irrevocably shifted towards this present course, with the Emperor Reborn at its helm, but we might still yet save the Original Universe which existed before Palpatine's Clone mettled in it."**

"**It may be prudent then, to assume this reality will persist even if we suceed. Making it even more important to placate Thrawn and determining if there are any other cloning facilities where another Emperor Reborn or Reapers could be creataed. Do you think Jade will be here? I would think she'd be at the Emperor's side and not languishing on some remote world.**"
**"High chance she'll be there. It's her base of operations, after all."**
**"If we get a clean data dump from N-Zee, we could mine that data to uncover other cloning sites potentially."**

"**Then we'll need to do everything possible to make sure NZ gets the code cylinder and the time he needs to get the data**" She nods to both of them. "**NZ, are you to get our ETA to Wayland?**" Directing her attention to Wraith she gives a small shrug. "**We should head to the bridge and report in person to Thrawn.**"

Showing up on the Bridge of the Chimaera 35 minutes later, Lexi and Wraith are greeted by the Admiral sitting in his Spotless White Command Chair, flanked by 4 Death Troopers, and no doubt the stealthy Rook was lurking around somewhere as well.
Thrawn turns to regard you, a steely gaze in his cindering eyes.

Lexi steps onto the bridge, her gaze going momentarily to the windows where she can see the hyperspace tunnel streak past. Once Thrawn turns to acknowledge their arrival she in turn gives him a small nod in greeting. "**Admiral,**" she says steadily in greeting.
**"Ah yes, Commodore Lexington Ardena. I like the ring of that. I have been expecting you."**
Lexi frowns at him at the change of her name. "**I'm honored you thought to promote us, Admiral.. but my name isn't Lexington.**"

Turning his chair fully to face both Wraith and Lexi, Thrawn steepled his fingers and took up a haughty imperial tone of impertinence: **"Your name shall be as I deem it, Commodore. If I wish to call you Pawn you will gladly acquiesce. Do not forget who it is you serve. The True Empire gave one of our best, Breggor Kryze, in service to your life or was that not so?"** The four Death Troopers at Thrawn's sides bristle at the name of their fallen compatriot, raising their rifles to guard position on their chests, still pointed at the ceiling however. Thrawn's fiery eyes flash more intensely crimson than before, cool rage burning in the Grand Admiral's demeanor. **"From this day forth to honor his memory you will change your name, officially, to suit the Decorum of this Institution."** Pausing, he collects his anger and forces a cooler tone, still stern but passionless: **"Do I make myself clear, Commodore Lexington Ardena?"**
Rook had somehow sharked up behind Lexi and was already holding a needle thin vibroblade to the back of her neck, without her ever having noticed until now.
Wraith stepped in between Rook and Lexi, having the blade point at his adam's apple instead.

Lexi keeps her calm as she meets Thrawn's fiery gaze and her head turns only slightly as she realizes the threat now behind her. "**There will be other ways to memorialize the sacrifice Breggor Kryze has made. Changing my name will not be one of them. And if this is an example of your... Decorum.. then you certainly not the commander I thought you to be.**"
A thin red tickle of blood had appeared, dripping down Wraith's neck. He gestured behind him to Lexi, **"Uh... Lexi-- I mean-- Lexington, now might not be the time for-- spirited debate."**
Of course the Ysalamiri Habitat built into Thrawn's command throne kept any Force abilities from working in its vicinity.
**"Hold, Rook."** Thrawn waves his hand dismissively.
The Stealthy Alien had withdrawn the blade, but still hadn't moved from his position.
**"I am always in a mood, for spirited debate."** Settling back in his chair, he relaxes his overall upper body and regards Lexi again.

Lexi had shifted momentarily to scowl at Wraith, her gaze coming to rest of the line of blood as it dripped down his neck. Her attention comes back to Thrawn as the threat from Rukh is temporarily brought to a halt. A thin set to her lips indicates a stubborn resolve at her decision to not debate the changing of her name.

**"Now then, perhaps you were of a mind to argue the point? By all means, let us discuss it. Last we spoke, I had explicitly given you one of my best men, a lifelong Son of the Empire, to represent my interests at your side in my stead. Now, you come before me, and dare present yourself without the Sole Man I Trust in your company. That is not a gesture I take lightly, Commodore. Although your Mission Record thus far is exemplary, your Tertiary Objective of returning with Upsilon-3 was still nonetheless a complete and utter failure. Something the True Empire does not need is... failures. You have been graciously promoted by me to the position of Commodore. My most trusted servants do not lie to me. I estimate a less than 4% chance that Kryze uncovered something fishy about you two Force Users and had to be silenced before he could make a report. For Kryze to miss a report is something that is up until now utterly unheard of. He was a True Servant of the Empire. He knew that only through Unity, can we achieve Strength and Peace. Now..."** He narrows his eyes at Lexi, **"Would you care to give me some sign to show that you are not in harmony with the Concept of Unity?"**

"**I agree Kryze's loss is a detriment to future endeavors, but because of his own choices we're able to stand before you now,**" she says evenly. They were protecting the memory of Breggor Kryze, though a part of her rebelled at the idea of concealing the truth regarding Breggor's final moments. He had afterall been transformed by the serum into a mutant abhoration.

**“Well put. Perhaps you do have a small future in naval politics, Commodore….”** Thrawn doesn't wait for a reply, taking her stunned silence and interjecting to introduce a completely new topic. **"Now then, to business. I would like to introduce both of you, Commodore Lexington and Captain Kai, to the singular individual who will be replacing Breggor Kryze from this day forth.”** Thrawn pushed a button at his Command Throne, allowing a bulkhead door to swoosh open at the back of the upper Bridge, this door usually led to the Admiral’s Personal Art Chamber. Stepping out, a soldier marched out professionally. He was adorned in heavy and ornate blue glossy armor plates, white cape, and centurion plume emanating from a delicately ornate T-visor Helm adorned with bronze decorative designs, almost appearing to look like the Prototypical Knight of Myth or Legend.

**“May I introduce… 

Captain Mith’rax’nuruodo, adopted son of the Mith Family. I don’t expect you to know much about Chiss Culture, Commodore, but I shall endeavor to give you a basic outline. The Chiss are organized at the very top, as very large noble families, and thus, acquire adoptives in order to grow their strength of numbers. Such often have need to serve in high government, military, and technocratic positions. Captain Thrax, is one such, merit-adoptive as we call them. He has chosen to serve The True Empire as one of its Imperial Knights. They are Marshalls of the Law and serve only Justice. Their military rank is outside of the ordinary Navy Chain of Command, though of course he has also been granted the Navy title of Captain as well. Thus, they are able to oversee the just rulership and execution of laws within the boundaries of The True Empire. Captain Thrax, will be accompanying you from now on, and administering the Full Strength and Support of the True Empire in my stead. You are to give him all the courtesies that Breggor Kryze received, and any others which he deems necessary. I have given him strict orders to stay within the bounds, he may not be permitted to order you, but he can at any point, step in and Act With The Full Authority of My Name, at any point in time, during which he thinks you may have betrayed the interests of the True Empire. That, in summation, is my decree. Do you both understand your Commands as I have given them to you?”**
Lexi glances over the armored newcomer, before turning her attention back to Thrawn. She's maintained her neutral expression as she quickly processes the orders. "**Yes, sir,**" she says at last.
Keeping his hand over his neck to staunch the bleeding, he adds in a raspy voice, **"Yes, Admiral."**
**"Good."** Thrawn looks pleased with himself. **"Captain Thrax you may take it from here."**
The Imperial Knight approaches before the duo. **"If you'll follow me, the Grand Admiral has given us leave to return to your ship and make ready for the assault on Wayland."**
Thrax doesn't seem to have an Imperial Accent, but his voice seems melodic and filled with gravitas.

**"Yes, I am very busy planning every little inch of the invasion. You are all key pawns in this final assault on the Emperor's Storehouse. All of his Secrets shall be laid bare before us, and bring the True Empire out of the ashes once more to rise in Conquest of this Galaxy."**

Lexi gives the Knight a nod. "**Very well.**" At Thrawn's declaration she forces herself to remail neutral; she was liking Wraith's plan to blow up the storehouse at the earliest opportunity more and more. "**Then we won't remain to distract you, Admiral.**" She gives the Admiral a formal Imperial salute, before turning on her heel and heading for the exit not waiting for Thrax to lead the way.
**"Hold, just a minute. You have not been dismissed."**
The entire bridge freezes.
**"Rook... if you wouldn't mind, giving Captain Kai the Antidote, so that he may not die this day from your blade."**

Almost reluctantly, the Animal-Alien Hybrid steps forward, seemingly out of nowhere but in fact from a slim shadow. In its clutches is a vial of anti-toxin, which dangles at the end of a leathery strap. Kai catches it with his closed fist, and mutters, **"Thanks..."**

**"Now... You may take your leave, Force Users. Perhaps you shall find something in common with my wayward cousin in the Mith. Rest well, I need you sharp and ready to fight at your peak performance when our invasion launches in... Oh what's the count now... 10 Hours 53 Minutes."**
The bustling on the bridge seems to resume at that.

Lexi stops and turns once more to face Thrawn and watches as the alien provides the antidote to the poison they hadn't realized was on his blade. Now finally dismissed, Lexi doesn't say another word, though she glances at Wraith momentarily as she heads for the exit.
Along the way, Thrax keeps a distance away from the duo, allowing Wraith and Lexi to talk.
**" The kriff was that all about? I thought Thrawn was one of the Good Guys..."**
"**I don't know, but it's a difinitive shift...**" Lexi says in low tones. "**Are you ok?**" she asks glancing at him and the wound the alien had inflicted on him.

**"I'll be alright, pretty sure I don't really need to drink this either..." ** He squints at the contents of the vial in his hand, **"My Regenerative Bloodline usually makes me immune to all poisons, but I didn't want Thrawn to know that. Or Rook for that matter..."**
Smiling, he gives the vial to Lexi: **"Why don't you keep it instead."**
**"It could be a very valuable Ace in the Hole."**
She returns the smile, their thoughts on the matter mirrored. "**Just let me know if you start to feel unwell... We don't know anything about the poison Thrawn's lackey uses.**"

Upon reaching the Antioch, Thrax paused at the threshold of the Boarding Ramp, inspecting the craft carefully, before boarding it after Lexi and Wraith had made their climb inside. Entering the Living Room, Thrax looked around and took in the sights of the discarded tank top from the previous night of festivities, as well as the remains of the hanging plants that had grown to encompass the entire ceiling and were threatening to crowd out anything else in the attic space.
Marching forward, he met with Lexi and Kai on the Bridge along with N-Zee.
"**NZ.. This is Captain Thrax, he'll be joining us in place of Sergeant Kryze on our venture from here on out,**" she says by introduction.
**"Greetings and Salutations. I am N-Zee, Stealth and Electronics Warfare Specialist. Welcome aboard the Antioch, sir."**

Regarding the Droid for a moment, Captain Thrax turned back to Lexi: **"Thank you. I do not seek to interrupt your Mission, Commodore. To clarify... I have no loyalty to Thrawn, but instead my Loyalty lies with the True Empire. The Rule of Law is the only thing that keeps this Galaxy from turning into a backward smattering of barbarians jumping around star systems and looting and pillaging everything they see fit."**
"**Thank you for your cander, Captain... has Thrawn told you what our,**" she indicates Wraith. "**Ultimate objective is on Wayland?**"
**"Yes, he has informed me that he seeks Cloning Technology, as well as any other military secrets of use to the True Empire's Goals."**
Lexi glances at Wraith for a moment, giving him a chance to interfere in what she's about to say next. "**That is the mission *he* has slated for us, though not our only goal.**"
**"True. Thrawn also mentioned that you have your own goals to accomplish within, something about assassinating the Emperor himself."**
**"And his bitch of a bride."**
**"Rather bloodthirsty for just the two of you..."**
**"Three now. Four if you count N-Zee."**
**"Pleasant disintegrations to all of you, gentlemen."**

"**Our plan is to infilatrate the base using Wraith's knowledge of Reaper protocols while the strike team keeps the main forces busy. Get in... secure as much as we can for Thrawn to collect and hope the base doesn't self destruct around us when they realize what we're doing.**"

**"Once again, I must remind you, that I do not work for Thrawn. I work for the interests of the citizens of the True Empire. This Emperor you are planning to kill, is well known to us as a tyrant and enemy of the people. He deserves to die, and Thrawn has already briefed me on your objectives. Personally... I find it all rather outlandish, the stories of Time Travel and changing of Histories."**

Lexi blinks for a moment as she processes what she heard were their orders and the words Thrax was imparting. Finally she says to mirror his cander: "**It doesn't matter to me if you believe it, only that you understand that stopping this clone *is* the reason I'm here.**"

Thrax seems to square off in front of Lexi at that, **"I see the rumors spreading aboard the Chimaera were not exaggerated. They say that a beautiful Jedi Princess has caught the attention of the Grand Admiral, alongside her emotionally unstable Reaper boyfriend. I think I see now, that this is indeed a Match Made in Hell, between two individuals displaying equal amounts of arrogance and ignorance."**
Wraith had narrowed his eyes and balled his fists at that.
Lexi sees Wraith tense and gives him a sharp look before returning her gaze back to the armored Chiss. "**I'm not so arrogant to not admit my ignorance... so why don't you enlighten us...**"

**"I think your plan to discover a Time Traveling Clone is optimistic. The Time Travel Bureau of the False Empire is a secretive one. You'll be lucky to find a Branch of it at Mount Tantiss. Usually their Time Travel rituals require specialized Temples, devoted to ancient extinct orders called Jedi and Sith. There, is where you will find your Clone... Princess."**
**"I thought you just said that Time Travel stories sound to you outlandish."**
**"Merely testing to see how much you actually knew."**
**"There was no need to reveal to you Imperial State Secrets, but since you are clearly somehow in the know... it would seem pointless to hide those away now."**

Lexi keeps her calm as Thrax reminds them of what they already knew. "**We're already aware of the Temples, but without a way to reliably find them, it would take too long to find the one they would use to send this clone to the past. Quite simply as crazy as it is... we're running out of time. It seemed a better course to find where the clone would be born. If the clone isn't on Mount Tantis then we would use whatever information we could to determine our next step.**"

**"I see that perhaps... I have misjudged you, Princess. Forgive me."** He bows formally to Lexi. **"Your plan makes sense, now that I hear it straight from the source. I will... help you, to kill this False Emperor before he managed to kill the True One. I swear it, as Truth and Honor is my Witness."**

Lexi let's out an exasperated breath. "**I'd appreciate it if you didn't call me that... Jedi. Captain. Commodore. Take your pick. I'm not royalty and I make no claim of such. Everyone picked up on the bloody nickname because of him,**" she jabs her chin in Wraith's direction. With that matter cleared up in her mind she returns to his pact. "**You say you don't work for Thrawn, but he's expecting you to make sure we uphold our agreement and report to him our doings. Hypothetically, if we must choose between our mission for Thrawn and stopping the clone, will you still choose to help us?**"
**"I will choose to fulfill the Mission, whether that helps you or not is irrelevant to me."**
**"Do you mean Thrawn's Mission or ours?"**
**"Both if I am able. If I am not, then the Mission that provides the most for the True Empire will be my number one priority."**
**"But you can clearly see how taking out the Emperor's False Clone takes priority over giving Thrawn his Cloning Tanks, right?"**
**"Only if the Clone is on Mount Tantiss."**

**"He'll be there. It's where I was born, the top secret Reaper Production factory. How many Top Secret Cloning Labs does the Empire Run? It would only make sense to have the highest security for one, centralized location."**
**"How do you plan on breaching this top level security, exactly."**
**"Uh, that's uh... We're still ironing out the wrinkles in that little detail... I mean, there's always a ventilation duct right?"**
**"Charming."**

He seems to freeze at that, directing his gaze in your direction but remaining silent, arms folded leaning against a wall in the background.

The cargo bay rocks as the hyperjump is completed. Heavy tubolasers discharge from the massive cannons of the Chimaera, vibrating the entire vessel.
Above head, the flat holoprojectors turn from red to blue and the engines spool up on the Lambda shuttle. Everyone takes a seat and strap their harnesses on.

Hovering about 50 meters off the ground, and coming to a slow hover over a tree-less jungle canopy clearing, the speeder bikes are deployed with efficiency as the rear ramp deploys, and the 14 bikes take off from the cargo bay, doors closing and the Shuttles continuing their previous course and heading.

A hologram of Thrawn's blue face appears. **"We have managed to subdue the orbital resistance forces, but I estimate that the Imperial Response shall be arriving from the nearest Sector Fleet within the next 24 standard hours, to dislodge us from our position. Thrawn's hologram wavers briefly as his voice changes pitch as if in modulation by some transient background interference: **"General Arctilius reports his rather loud stompy AT-AT Assault Division is already moving, which means they'll be on high alert. Keep that in mind. Thrawn out."**

The last of the Speeder bikes are moved underneath tree cover.
The group sets out in two single file lines, each representing the White Doves or Nighthawks.
The White Doves begin to settle into a steady rhythm through the jungle.
Lexi makes arrangements for Ghost and Binoc to scout ahead to make sure they don't run into any surprises.
The two single file lines parallel their journey through the jungle.
In the first hour, nothing of importance changes.
Towards the beginning of their second hour of walking, Lexi begins to feel a gnawing feeling in the pit of her stomach.
Lexi frowns as the pit in her stomach begins to grow. She casts a furtive a glance around. "**Something's not right,**" she says quietly to Wraith next to her.

One of the large boulders that the White Doves were passing off to one side, suddenly comes alive and unfurls to its full, monstrous bulk. It growls a deep, earthly growl that causes a miniature tremor, knocking some of the troopers off their feet.
As it smashes its massive rocky arms into the ground, chunks of rock fly in every direction like shrapnel.
The flying rocks punch into Team C, eliciting cries of pain from Grave, Skinker and Blaze.

Lexi shields her face as the flying shrapnel scatters about them and the cries of her men's pain brings her attention to a sharp focus. Looking upon the creature whose territory they had encroached upon she reaches out to the Force. The rockbeasts rage at their presence fills her and she pushes it aside as she takes one step and then another. Her hand oustretches as she sends waves of a calming energy towards the beast through the Force. *I'm sorry... we'll leave. We're not staying.* While she knows the beast can't understand her words, it can at least understand her feelings, the impressions she sends with the words, of them leaving the beast to his peaceful slumber. The large beast blinks at her and she blinks back, her outstretched hand now just a breath away from touching the rocky skin. It hunches down, calming from its rage and continues to consider Lexi. She doesn't turn as she calls back in low tones to the men behind her. "**Get the men moving... slowly. We don't want to startle him again.**" Remaining in place, she waits for everyone to move on before at last moving from in front of the rockbeast and following the last of the men.
The rocky beast bends down and sniffs at Lexi, creating a wave that washes over her face. It licks her cheek and turns around, waddling away back into the undergrowth, stomping its chunky feet.

Lexi can't help but watch the beast with wonder as she feels its breath on her cheek followed by the wetness it leaves as it turns away. She continues to watch it until it disappears and then follows her men as they continue their journey. Hurrying her pace, she moves to pass each of them, checking their wounds as she goes. When she catches up to Wraith, she finally lets out a breath. "**Let's find a place to make a stop and see to the injured. Between myself and Medic Zess we'll make quick work.**"
After a quick 15 minutes rest, the wounds are patched up and luckily nobody's leg was injured so everyone could keep walking.
The men were definitely giving Lexi odd looks now, keeping their distance and speaking in hushed tones.

Lexi pretends she doesn't notice the change in their demeanor towards her, but it doesn't leave her any less concerned. What she had done had been strange and unexpected, even with them knowing what she was. She had shown power and they had been condition to recognize those with powers to be Reapers. As she finishes bandaging the last of Grave's wounds she gives him a reassuring smile. "**There you go, Trooper... Good as new.**"
**"Thanks... Commander... I mean it."**
**"If only I had been quicker to raise my shield... none of my men would have gotten hurt."**

She moves to stand and move away from Grave, brushing away the leaf litter that had collected on her pants. "**None of us saw the beast until it was too late. We all did what could under the circumstances.**"
**"Generous, but untrue. I saw you reacting before it even burst out."**
**"Perhaps changing our designation to White Dove wasn't the boneheaded move so many thought it to be, after all..."**
**"Yeah tell me about it, otherwise what else did we all just witness?"**
"**I was not quick enough to recognize the danger to warn the rest of you,**" she says apologetically.
**"They say sometimes reality rhymes, you know, like poetry..."**
**"Speaking of warning us, what happened to Ghost and Binoc? Where were those guys asleep at the wheel or something?"**
**"Sorry, we walked past the boulder just like you did Blaze. I was more on the lookout for Imperials, not wildebeasts."**
**"Hmph, I did my job as I was commanded to. The rock posed no danger when I encountered it on the path first, Blaze. Be our guest and take point if you wish. I won't complain."**
Lexi makes a quick motion to call their attention and silence them.
**"By the way, while you guys were getting patched I found something--"** He halts and looks up at Lexi.

Lexi nods to Binoc but first decides to address the accusations they had been casting. "**In the wilds of worlds such as this, it's hard to determine what will be a threat. One creature that appears docile when faced against one intruder may not find them much of a threat. Our group is large and it wanted to defend its territory.**" She looks between them, ending any sort of further arguments in regards to the creature before turning to Binoc. "**What did you find, Binoc?**"

**"Well uh... A couple of things actually. First of all, during the brief encounter, I was able to set my visor to a certain frequency, and from now on I should be able to identify the pattern on any future 'rocks' we walk past. Second... up ahead Ghost and I found a perimeter, we don't think we managed to alert the Imperials. There's basically an energy-fence up ahead, emanating from a Watchtower. The Watchtower seems to be holding the Power Generator, and we're pretty sure its abanonned. Someone is going to have to sneak inside and simulate an accidental power failure if we want to proceed."**
**"Master, might I suggest this could be a job for a certain stealth droid?"**

Lexi turns to NZ and gives him a nod. "**Of course, NZ. Perhaps you should take Bonic or Ghost with you as support.**" She looks at the pair and raises an eyebrow to see if one of them would volunteer for the task.
**"You own a stealth commando droid, Commander? This day is just getting more and more interesting..."**
**"I work alone. I don't need no stupid clanker to sneak into an abandonned power shed."**
"**But do you have the expertise to make the sabatoge appear accidental?**" she asks Ghost.

**"Actually, sabotage is one of my strengths. Maybe I'll set off an electrical fire by pouring some water onto the generator, who knows if a crack develops and water leaks onto some sensitive electrical equipment."**

"**Grand... I'm glad you've volunteered then... to accompany NZ to the Watchtower.**" Her attention turns to NZ. "**While you're there, see if you can gather any intelligence regarding any additional surprises we may encounter on the way.**"
**"Certainly, Master. I'll try and access the records within the Watchtower, perhaps we can obtain a map of the enemy base."**
"**Don't take any risks which may draw attention towards us,**" she warns. **"We still need to get to our breach points without being noticed.**"
**"Don't worry Master, you organics never expect a droid to be anything but an obedient servant."**
**"Wait... is that thing going to battle alongside us? I certainly hope it's been properly leash-coded."**
**"Don't worry your squishy little brains over it, Trooper. I have all the requisite paperwork on file with my Master."**
**"On second thought... Pairing it up with Ghost somehow just feels so right..."**

Lexi is reminded suddenly of the incident on Dantooine and she frowns. "**Ghost. NZ. Make your preparations and then make your way to this Watchtower. Before you leave, let Commander Bishop or Captain Kai know when we should expect you back.**"
**"Yes ma'am... Give me 20 minutes and I'll have that fence down... Commander."**
**"Master, I'd be delighted to do this task for you, finally a demonstration of my superlative abilities..."**
Both Droid and Human walk off and activate holocloaks, disappearing into the jungles like in the movie Predator.

Lexi gives a small shake of her head and then turns to the other men gathered around. "**Rest up... we'll be moving again when they return.**" Standing she starts to head towards the camp of the Nighthawks, to catch Wraith up on the existance of the Watchtower and the mission she had sent NZ and Ghost on. "**I need to meditate,**" she admits to him, her tiredness from the stunt she had pulled with the beast finally catching up to her.

Ghost arrived at the edge of the red energy barrier, and stopped short just before the jungle canopy ended. A bolt of lightning split the sky, reflecting off the silver alloy of his Shadow Armor. N-Zee was chattering something next to his ear the whole trip, incessantly and with the absent-mindedness of a University professor.

Ghost reasoned that they could climb atop the Watchtower and infiltrate through an access hatch, activating his camo holostealth mode, he sets out into the pouring rain. He knew the raindrops would distort his optical camouflaged, but it couldn't be helped. On the plus side, the rain would greatly muffle any noises he or the chattering droid would make.

While the droid had simply clattered up the side of the building like a mechanical spider, Ghost had to utilize a grappling gun to impact the roof and had scaled up the building by walking along its side. By the time Ghost had arrived, N-Zee had already burned through the maintenance hatch lock using his fusion torch and was crawling through the ductwork inside the Watchtower in the style of a mechanized arachnid. Ghost grunted, pulling up over the side and elbow crawling through the ducts, depositing them both into the control room with muffled thuds. Ghost activated his search light on his right temple and looked around.
**"Access found: Slicing mainframe now... first attempt."**
N-Zee had his finger plugged into the Scomplink.
**"Access Denied."**

**"Guess we're going to do this the old fashioned way..."** Ghost sets an ion detonator down next to the circuit wall panel that has the most electrical signs plastered around it, programming the timer to go off by remote signal.
**"N-Zee, you need to put the second ion detonator next to the main computer, just in case."**

Ghost and N-Zee manage to set up the sabotage and sneak their way out of the Watchtower, making their way back to Camp. N-Zee was caught on camera, or at least his outward form did, under optical camo, but the Stormtroopers in the control room were too relaxed to notice.

Walking back to the meditating form of Lexi, Ghost clicks his heels: **"Ghost reporting for duty, ma'am, we're all set up. Once we reach the perimeter, we can detonate the ion charges to give a temporary pause in the energy fencing, before the computer reboots and backup power supply flicks on. Judging by the version of software they were using, probably give us 2 minutes without power to pass through the energy fence."**
**"I wasn't able to slice the mainframe, Master. I failed, I apologize. As I said before, I would need the proper military codes to slice in."**

Having taken refuge from the rain against the trunk of one of the trees whose dense foliage was sufficient to block the worst of the rain, Lexi blinks up at the newly returned trooper and droid. Slowly, she lets out a breath as her thoughts come back into focus to the present. Standing she looks over the pair. "**It was worth a try, NZ. We knew it would be difficult even with the codes. What's important is we make it through the fence without being detected. Well done.**" She looks over Ghost for a moment and then gestures towards the others who had been taking the time as she had commanded to rest. **"Go take 5, Ghost. You deserve it.**"
Without saying a word, he turns around and walks off.

**"There were camera units inside the control room of the Watchtower, they may have caught a glimpse as I was planting the detonator there, we should move before they realize what happenned and play back the tape."**

Lexi nods and motions towards Bishop and Wraith to join her so she can fill them in. "**Break camp,**" she orders at the end of her update. "**When we get to the permiter we should break into our smaller teams to ensure we can make it through the defensive line with all haste.**"
The whole camp gets up, folding up their makeshift tents and tarps, breaking down and putting out fires.
After a 5 minute forced march, the two commando teams reach the energy fence, humming with menace.
Raindrops sizzle off the red energy barrier as they land.

Lexi stands with the other 4 members of her team and looks up and down the line of the fence to make sure the other groups were ready to proceed. Confident she nods to Ghost. "**Blow it,**" she orders and gets ready to move.

**"Fire in the hole."** Pressing a button on his vambrace, immediately in the distance the lights of the Watchtower flicker and go out, followed very quickly by the level below it, and the level below that. The humming of the red energy fence dies down, making a power-down sound and the sizzling cooking raindrops stop their hissing noise.

The two commando squads start climbing the de-powered barrier fence, one of two troopers straddling the top of the fence and helping people to climb over more effectively. The Commanders are urging everyone on and hurrying everybody up, and in the end, thanks to no serious injuries among the men, everyone makes it across safely in time. The Watchtower lights flick on once more, and the fence powers up again, although this time, the Commando Teams are on its opposite side. The outer perimeter had been infiltrated successfully. There would probably be a report filed somewhere, and given the battle in orbit, someone would be dispatched to find out what happened here, but only 20 minutes remained to reach the actual Reaper stronghold, and very likely by the time any technicians arrived the attack would be well underway and some minor security breach would be the least of the Base Commander's concern.

Lexi lets out a breath of relief as they make it over just in time for the fence to come alive once more behind them. "**Let's get a move on,**" she looks over at Wraith, realizing the time for them to split up was coming fast upon them. Sidling up to him as they continue on she murmers in low tones. "**It's nearly time,**" she says simply and then giving him her small smile adds. "**May the Force be with you.**"
The scouts detect a routine patrol, and after 2 minutes of waiting, it passes and the commandos resume their march.
The body of Mount Tantiss is obscured by rain and foliage, but once in a while its greak bulk is glimpsed in the background.

Lexi pulls the hood of her cloak forward to deter the unrelenting rain and shivers as a sudden feeling of deja vu overcomes her: the vision she'd once had of the ominous mountain and the dark secrets that lie within.  With the signal to pause to wait for the enemy patrol to pass, her thoughts seem to drift and she has to make an effort to come back to the present. 
It's with relief they start to move once more, drawing ever closer to their objective.
Binoc appears from ahead on the path, stopping in the middle of the path and walking in the opposite direction. Something seems to be off in his stride.

Lexi calls a halt as Bonic comes into view anticipating another obstacle up ahead he was about to warn them of. However as he comes closer, a familiar sensation of dread causes a pit in her stomach. She's on edge as her hand automatically goes to her lightsaber, but she doesn't light it. "**That's not Binoc,**" she declares to anyone nearby. "**Be on guard.**" Taking the initiative, she moves to intercept the Not Binoc before he can reach their main force.
Several rifles go up and some of the commandos crouch taking up positions in cover as Lexi suddenly takes off in a dash towards the Scout Trooper.
Ignites a purple lightsaber suddenly in one hand. Instead of Binoc, a new female voice emanates: **"I guess there's no fooling you is there..."**

Lexi's green blade she had at the ready in her hand ignites in response, shortly followed by her yellow blade. "**Unfortunately for you... Where's my trooper?**" she demands. Stalling the disguised Reaper long enough for Wraith to catch up.
Wraith had been right there alongside Lexi the whole time, igniting a single cyan blade, handle made of intricate wood instead of metal.
**"... hoo hooooo... How unfortunate for *you*."**
**"Don't worry, little Binoc is still alive, sleeping. While he was relaxing... I slurped up his delicious memories, and now I know all about you, Jedi Knight Lexi Ardena."**
"**You only know what Binoc knows...**" Lexi says evenly, not allowing this Reaper to perterb her. "**And he doesn't know everything.**"
He lifts a comm to his lips, whispering: **"Begin jamming now, right now."**
**"Jamming? Oh no I want to save your delicious deaths all to myself... well myself, and my partner, at least..."**
Stepping off to one side, Binoc reveals another individual immediately several meters behind him who had stepped out of the foliage.

Lexi shifts her body continuing to follow the Not Binoc Reaper, expecting Wraith to take the cue to move his focus to the newcomer. Her breathing remains even, calm in the face of the two trained killers. "**Only the two of you?**" she asks, drawing out the question as if bored.

Binoc's stormtrooper armor suddenly turns into black smoke, that slowly in lazy puffs waives away from the Reaper, broken up by the rain, revealing the Reaper's alien humanoid shape, still clutching the purple saber.
**"More than enough... Permit me to make introductions. I am Sheethe, and this is my associate, Voctann. We couldn't help but notice you were hopelessly lost..."**
"**What I notice... is you and your companion are in our way.**"
Voctann's voice comes out reverberating electronically harsh and grating, from behind a thick face mask: **"THIS ONE... IS MOCKING US... REMOVE HER TONGUE."**
**"How about I go over there and crack open your egg head, you talking red cue-ball."**
**"Your friend is virile, I can smell his scent upon you... This must be a mated pair."**

Lexi can't help but roll her eyes at Sheethe's assessment. "**Mates... no. Though his virility is only rivaled by his skill in combat...**" she says lightly, flourishing her green blade in front of her. "**I'm sure he'd be happy to demonstrate,**" she adds coyly, continuing her mocking banter. Lexi risks a sideways look over at Wraith. Time was wasting bantering with the pair, they couldn't afford it with the clock ticking for when they needed to be in position. Her attempts to goad the Reapers into attacking them were taking too long. Counter to her usual methods of waiting for her opponent to reveal their weaknesses, she charges at Sheethe.

The Reaper twists deflecting the first green saber deftly, but the second slides through and burns a cauterized mark on the Reaper's torso. Sheethe's visage turns ghastly and terrifying. **"You *scarred* me... you shall pay for that."**
With her left hand, Sheethe takes out a small blaster pistol from her hip holster and squeezes the trigger at Lexi.

The blaster bolt shot out far wide, but somehow impacted something behind Lexi, and bounced back to hit Lexi in the back of the head perfectly aimed... until the young Jedi managed to deflect the blaster bolt with the very tip of her green blade, by winding her arm behind her back, right before the bolt struck Lexi's skull.
**"Oops... teehee, looks like I hit your friend."**
Wraith had charged Voctann the same time as the Jedi had engaged the female Reaper.

After Wraith's cyan blade impacted on Voctann's helmet, the blade sparked and dissipated, leaving Wraith clutching a useless sparkling lifesaber wood handle. He was confused, but didn't have much time to contemplate his actions, as Voctann had swung a stone-segment-staff with a far-reaching swipe, making Wraith bend over backwards and extend his chin to avoid getting clobbered. The segments of the staff were connected with red saber energy, allowing the staff to bend and weave like a spry branch with every strike.

The armored Reaper was running alongside Wraith and whipping his heavy stone staff at Wraith's agile body every which way, leaving hard impact strikes on the ground, but never actually touching the Black-clad Sith turned True Jedi.
**"... I SHALL CRUSH YOU ... DIE WEAKLING ... DIE ..."**
Lexi calls on the Force, reaching out her hand before jerking it to one side. At her bidding the blaster pistol the Reaper had just fired, flies from her hand to disappear into the underbrush.

Catches a strangely powerful blaster pistol bolt in his shoulder pad, which spins him around. All around, both commando squads are training their rifles on the two melee engagements, occassionally firing off wide shots meant to avoid hitting Lexi and Wraith.

Sheethe hisses at Lexi, cursing in an alien language with invective. Wiggling her fingers in the air, the Reaper tries to enact some kind of Sith Sorcery, but it seems to not effect Lexi in the least, who stands determined to interpose herself between the Reaper and her Commandos.
Spitting with another alien curse on her lips, Sheethe darts into the undergrowth, disappearing from sight.
Reaching out with her Green Sight, Lexi can sense where the lithe Reaper is, roughly, and realizes she's jumping up one of the jungle trees with surprising speed from one branch to the next.
Wraith's fists had been engulfed in violet flames, as he had started deflecting the stone-section-saber-staff with his bare forearms and fists, since he was lacking a functioning weapon.

A muffled electronic laughter resounded from the helm, amplified artificially: **"NOW LETS SEE ... YOU DEFLECT *THIS*! ..."** Extending one palm forward, Voctann's red glove made a fist and twisted upward, at the same time as a spike of stone split up from the ground and nearly skewered Wraith underneath his chin, had he not brought himself short in time. Voctann made a claw gesture, and spikes of rock emerged from the newly formed stalagmite, nearly impaling Wraith who had to backflip multiple times to avoid getting speared.

The armored crimson Reaper began to mutter ancient Sith language: **"EKHERAK NAGATH SOTHOS!"** His eye lenses turned red and shot forth in a twin beam that scarred the landscape with not damage, but a line of newly formed red rocks. Amongst them, Wraith's leg was caught up to the knee.
**"HA HA HA ... TRAPPED NOW TRY ... EVADING THIS TIME ..."**

Wraith yelled out a howling piercing cry that split the night, and brought his forearm in a karate chop to crack the stone in half, freeing his leg just in time as where he stood before, a dozen spikes had emerged from the surrounding perimeter of earth and all met where Wraith's center mass had stood just an instant before.

Tracking the Reaper into the trees, the woman glowing green in Lexi's vision as she proceeds to climb higher, Lexi draws inward summoning the life around her and encouraging it to grow. Branches and vines come to Lexi's call as the Verdant Force flows through her. Vainly the Reaper slashes at the vines with her purple lightsaber as they attempt to strangle her, but even as they snag her she begins to chant. The vines and branches collapse on themselves as their target turns into smoke and escapes their grasp. 
Lexi's eyes flash cyan once more as she reaches out with the Force to try and locate the Reaper, but frustratingly, she can find no sign.

Voctann notices out of one side of his vision that Lexi is summoning up another Verdant Push, as the plants extent towards the armored Reaper, he executes a circular whirl of his stone saber staff, which cuts the plants like a blender.
Leaping backwards using the force, he ends up standing atop a stone stalagmite and assesses his opponents.
**"Open fire!"**
A withering hail of blaster fire, slugthrower, and even a jet of fire extends forward at the Reaper.
The Reaper jumps down behind the stalagmite, taking cover.

**"GETHASHNOS... ALOK ... SYTHIPHIS!"** The earth starts to rumble, causing some of the troopers to stumble and shoot wildly in the wrong direction. 1d6 Perfect cones of red stone rise into the air and begin to shoot at the squads.
**"The Togruta is up in that tree over there, I'll take this cue-ball headed guy, trust me lightsabers don't seem to work on him..."**

The first perfect red cone of stone is sliced in half by Lexi, both halves split off in opposite directions and impact the underbrush. Wraith was meant to receive the second stone spike, executing a perfect roundhouse kick and deflecting the rock with a loud crash. The last two spikes were aimed at Leaper, the frontmost of the Troopers. A roar of a jetpack is heard, as Leaper ascends and shoots off like a rocket, meanwhile two red stone spikes skewer the tree behind him.
There seems to be a temporary break in the action, as the Troopers cease fire and the enemy Reapers have taken up cover positions.
Lexi looks over in the direction in which Wraith indicates the female Reaper has retreated. "**You don't think they're calling for reinforcements do you?**" she asks under her breath to him.
**"If they decide to turn their backs to me, that'll be the last mistake of their lives... I think we should tell the men to move on without us though..."**
**"These are very strong opponents, they outclass normal troopers, it would seem."**
"**I agree... we need them in position to breach.. we can always follow them once we're done here.**"
Wraith's eyes gleam cyan as he assesses the enemy's position in the Force, **"They don't seem to be set on retreating, if their auras are any indication."**
Wraith turns back to his squad, whispering loudly: **"Nighthawks, we'll hold the enemy here, you need to complete the mission... I'll rejoin you once we've dealt with these two clowns."**
Lexi turns to her own men and gives similar orders, adding:"**See if you can't find Binoc on the way. If you can't I'll locate him before I rejoin you.**
Making hand gestures, he makes a rodeo gesture with his index finger and points into the woods, taking them away from the combat. **"White Doves, on me."**
**"Nighthawks, move out."**
After a few seconds, the squads disappear into the rain and foliage.
After a few beats, Voctann walks calmly out of cover. He is slowly twirling his staff in one outstretched hand and walking towards Lexi and Wraith.
**"I'LL ENJOY IT... WHEN I HUNT THEM ... THEIR DEATHS SHALL BE SLOW."**
It seems that Round 2 was about to begin imminently, but the Reapers were drawing this out to enjoy it more.

From somewhere in the a high pitched laughter is heard, **"It seems they really came here thinking to take on the Reapers, I wonder if they lost a bet? That's got to be the stupidest thing anybody ever's tried to pull."**
**"EITHER VERY BRAVE ... OR VERY STUPID ... I"M BETTING THE LATTER."**
**"They seem to be under the auspices of a certain Grand Admiral Thrawn, a self-styled Imperial warlord. There's a large reward for the blue traitor's head, perhaps these two can lead us to it."**

**"You probably don't recognize me, but I used to be a Reaper too, until I realized it was essentially glorified slavery. Why not join us and Grand Admiral Thrawn instead? Don't you want to be free one day?"**
**"SO ... YOU ARE A TRAITOR WHO MUST BE DESTROYED."**
**"Yeah, this is why I left..."**

Lexi stands at the ready, letting Wrath attempt to convince the other Reapers. "**The Empire is on the decline... What do you think will happen to you? We're offering an opportunity to make something more of yourselves than being under the thumb of the Emperor and his Queen.**
**"DIE REBEL SCUM"**
He sprints forward, his staff leading like an Olympic pole vaulter, using the staff to catch air time and come down slamming the staff with a doublehanded overhand strike.

As Wraith interposes himself to protect Lexi, he crosses his arms to take the staff directly on his fiery forearms. Unfortunately the wind up was so powerful that Wraith's forearm bone snaps. Clutching his broken arm in his other hand, Wraith attempts to execute two roundhouse kicks.
Voctann's helm gets rung once, then twice, the Reaper losing his form and stumbling as if dizzied by the thundering back to back kicks.

A purple sniper bolt flies out of the canopy down at Lexi, well aimed and faster than the eye can see almost. Using the Force to memontarily slow down time, Lexi deflects the bolt at Voctann instead. Voctann doesn't bother to block as it hits his armor, leaving a black scortch mark on his forehead.

Voctann watches as Lexi lifts a log with telekinesis and throws it at the Reaper. Thrusting his forearm down, palm facing the soil, a rumble is heard in reaction as a stone slab rises from the ground to form a shield blocking the flying log.

Another bolt flies out of the canopy aimed for Lexi, but with another expert twirl of the blade, it gets redirected at Voctann once more, this time the purple sniper bolt had burned in between red armor plates, eliciting a snarling electromodulated cry of pain from the Armor Reaper.

Reaching out with the Force, Lexi lifts one of the boulders Voctann has conventiently created and hurls it towards the tree canopy where she spots the hiding Reaper. Proving true, the unsuspecting Sniper is unable to avoid the projectile and falls from her hiding place to the ground.

Wraith had mended his broken arm, regenerative powers reknitting the bones slowly. Changing the flow of his Spirit Energy, he coalesced the violet flow of energy into a ball the size of a basketball. Sending it at Voctann the sphere exploded in a blinding flash of purple and expanded outwards, burning away Voctann's flesh inside his armor. A smoking pile of black charred armor plates is all that remains of the formiddable Reaper, staff dropping to the ground alongside.
Hitting several branches of on the way down, Sheethe encanted Sith Sorcery and disappeared into a puff of smoke just short of impacting the ground, and was gone.
**"I doubt she'd show her face after losing to us, probably went to lick her wounds..."**

Lexi curses under her breath as she realizes the other Reaper has teleported away once more. "**You don't think she'll go raise an alarm?**" Bending over the charred remains of the armor, she pokes through to see if the Reaper has anything useful... say a code cylinder with even limited security access.
**"Heh, no she knows as well as I that failure equals death. Once the other Reapers learn she retreated, she'll be 'recycled' and cloned again."**
Unfortunately all electronics had been burned by the Spirit Sphere detonation.
Lexi shakes her head. "**It amazes me any of the Reapers would follow such.**" She stands and brushes her pants off. "**Thank you for taking that blow earlier... How's your arm?**"
**"Healing nicely. Let's catch up to our men."**
Bending down, he picks up the staff and tests swinging it.
"**Is your lightsaber irrepairable?**"
**"I think it shorted out, something about the stone in his armor did that..."**
**"N-Zee, carry that armor would ya."**

Lexi raises an eyebrow. "**Taking trophies now?**" Shifting back to her earlier thought she rest her hand on the hilt of her yellow saber. "**If you don't think you can repair it in time. You could use one of mine.**"
**"Thanks but... my saber would be very mad if I cheated on it with yours. Besides..."** He hefts the staff in one arm, **"Trophies are nice to have."**

Lexi cocks her head and then just shakes it. "**Suit yourself. I would hope it would understand I just want you to be safe**" She grins at him and then starts heading down the path. "**I wonder if Bishop found Binoc...**"
After 5 minutes of trekking, the duo is able to catch up to the Force-void bubbles, and learn that Binoc was unconscious and being carried on Jumbo's back.
**"Commander... is everything alright?"**
**"Sir, welcome back."**

Lexi steps up to join Bishop and glances behind her for a moment at NZ carrying the scorched armor of their adversary. "**We got one, but Sheethe got away. Captain Kai doesn't think she'll trouble us though.**"
"**Everything well with the men?**"
**"Commander, that Witch did something to Binoc, he can't wake up for some reason."**

"**She said she put him to sleep...**" Lexi goes over to where Binoc has been slung over Jumbo's back to get a better look at the sleeping trooper. "**I'll need to get out of the bubble with him if it's Force induced.**"
**"Deactivate jamming, there is no longer any need."**
Jumbo sets Binoc down against a tree.
**"He gonna make it ya think Commander?"**
