"**Fall back to the tunnels.**"

Backtracking down to one level of tunnels the Squad seems a bit unhappy about reverting the inevitable progress towards the return to civilization, but they also comply having faced Reapers just a few hours before and none too eager to repeat the experience. Unfortunately, the Reaper seems to have some tracking abilities of its own, as you sense that the Reaper Signature is following you at a distance, though certainly not with perfect efficiency, and it seems to weave in and out of conscious detection now, as if the recipient is aware that somehow it is being tracked in the currents of the Force.

After a tense few moments of playing cat and mouse, Lexi abruptly turns a corner and runs full on into the Reaper headed up the passageway in the opposite direction. The Reaper had been making no noise, even though it must have been walking through moving water. The Reaper bears a red lightsaber, dipping its tip in the water in surprise at running into Lexi apparently. A small mist appears, then the saber momentarily lifts and stops creating additional steam. From the misty fog, the Reaper steps out and reveals her form. She is a strikingly handsome Zeltron female, wearing form-fitting magenta leather and short black cape. The Reaper has a friendly smile on her face and her big eyes blink in excitement at finding someone else wading through the sewage here with her. Her lips part and she smiles, blinking rapidly.
**"Excuse me but have you seen my pet Grych?"**

Lexi blinks in surprise at having now running into the Reaper after their attempts to avoid her. "**Not recently,**" she replies slowly. "**He's around here somewhere.**" Her hand extends behind her signalling obliquely for Bishop to begin pulling the others back the way they had come. "**Has he been gone long?**" She asks trying to keep the Reaper's attention on her rather than the squad coming up behind her. "**I wasn't expecting anyone else to be drudging their way through this mess.**"

**"Huh?"** She blinks rapidly again, turning her head to one side and inspecting Lexi as if for the first time. **"Wait do I know you? You don't look like a Reaper, but you're clearly Force sensitive. What's your name? Are you some kind of new Project Designation?"**
Thanks to the misty fog, it appears that the soldiers are able to fade away from Lexi as ordered, thanks to her staying out of Ysalamiri range and ahead in front by several meters.
She grins, **"Oh wait, I get it... Sheethe is that you? Up to your old tricks again?"**
**"You brought a stormtrooper squad down here why exactly? Pfff... whatever. If you don't know where Grych is, then he's gone a different direction entirely..."**
**"I can't believe this is how I'm spending my afternoon, trekking through sewage... bleh."**
**"It's Nys. Nys Kurana you remember me right?"**

"**Nys... right,**" Lexi says noncommittally. "**This definitely hasn't been the highlight of my day.**" Indicating the way she had come she adds, "**He's not down here. He must have gone down another passage.**"

Nys narrows her eyes at Lexi, noticing something is off. She hesitates then asks, **"Wait a sec, do you know what this drill is on about? Been blaring those sirens full blast like the past half hour... You've certainly found a good place to get away from it all..."**

Lexi grins at Nys, mimicking the best she can the expression she had seen on Sheethe. "**Your guess is as good as mine. I haven't heard anything other than a call to Z9. Have you heard anything about that?**"
**"Maybe... but, you're not Sheethe, you're way too nice to be Sheethe. So the question is... Who are you?"** She narrows her eyes conspiratorially and smiles.
She seems to take note of weapons hanging at Lexi's belt with a survey of her gaze.

Lexi knows the sight of the two lightsabers wasn't going to raise any suspicions and so far she's made no motion to draw them, but with the Reaper's appraising look at her belt Lexi realizes she was losing her attempt to get information from the Reaper. She'd never been very good at lying and her game of words was coming to an end. "**I never said I was Sheethe, you did.**" Lexi gives the Zeltron a coy smile. "**You and I.. we've never met.**"
She nods, as if expecting that much. **"Alright, if you're not Sheethe, then just who are you exactly? Some kind of new model?"** She pouts, looking over Lexi's body voraciously.
Lexi smirks at the Reaper as she puts a hand on her hip and poses under her appraisal.  "**Jealous?**"
**"Maybe a little... What's your Designation?"**
"**Classified.**"

**"You're no fun, Classified. Suit yourself. I'm tired of this stinking tunnel maze, and I plan to take a shower before slipping into something more comfortable..."** Her gaze lingers over Lexi, **"You're welcome to join me for both, if you've nothing better to do... Miss Classified."**
She turns halfway making ready to leave seemingly.
"**Tempting. Perhaps later.**" Lexi gives the Reaper a sly grin. "**I may even bring a friend.**"

**"If you ever change your mind, my Comm will be open. See ya round..."** Backing up slowly and not turning her back even for an instant, Nys walks back steadily, plunging her saber and creating mist once more, disappearing around a corner.

Lexi watches as the Reaper leaves and tracks her through the Force to see if she'd double back a different way through the maze. It seemed to her the Reaper was sincere about leaving, but she wouldn't put it past the Reaper to be lying.
After a long moment observing the Reaper's movements Lexi backtracks to catch up with her squad.

Bishop rejoins after a few beats, **"Commander, you're pretty damn good at handling Reapers gotta say, I initially had my doubts, but that's two now you've managed to drive away. What are your orders ma'am?"**
He holds up his rifle across his chest in parade salute stance.
Sector Z4 seems to be clear of Reaper influence, although Lexi can sense random stormtrooper patrols every 5 minutes or so passing through, they can easily be heard from far away and avoided.

Lexi gives the commander a self gratified smile. She'd manage to divert the Reaper without even drawing her lightsaber. 
"**Where you able to send Ghost out to scout?**"
**"Over here, Commander."** Ghost had appeared behind Lexi.
**"Just got back from another round of crawling through pipes, ma'am. There isn't another exit other than at Z4 I'm sorry to say."**
**"Magnet couldn't pull a data dump so I just went to the all the rooms and read the numbers scrawled over each door panel. The manual way still works, turns out."**
Lexi pats her lightsaber. "**Fortunately I have universal door.  Getting out isn't a concern. How far into the level did you get?**"
**"Z23, after that point the pipe narrows too much."**

Lexi nods as she looks at the map. "**We can use the tunnel then to avoid confronting two of the Reapers, but there's still a Reaper or two on the way.**" She points to the ladder going to B2. "**Unless we determine otherwise, this should be our goal. Though once we get topside we're more likely to come across enemy squads.**"
**"There's also a turbolift at Z7 but I can't access it without proper codes Commander."**
Lexi considers. "**We're going to need to find an officer. A Reaper may have what we need, but it's a risk to confront one.**"
**"Oh and sorry that's actually a set of stairs, not a ladder, I mislabelled that. It's still got a door I can't slice at one end, but as you said... you've got the universal key."**
**"There's Stormtrooper Commanders leading the patrols that routinely make rounds on this level, they might have a lower level security key that could at least give us access to higher floors."**
**"Statement: I can attempt to slice the door, but the security up until now has been top notch, I estimate only 5% chance of success without a Code Cylinder to assist my slicing programs."**
**"There are several protocol droids, Gonk droids, and mouse droids on this level. I can attempt to mimic one of the protocol droids if I am discovered."**
**"They all seem to be congretated around the power generators, especially the Gonk droids."**
"**Power generators... What are they powering...**" She asks no one in particular. An idea coming to mind.
Her thoughts turn back to the task. "**Are you an NZ up for some more sabotage? I want to know what those generators are powering and if could benefit us if they were no longer working...**"

**"I'd have to have Magnet on standby to try slicing it, or this droid I suppose, but we aren't likely to get more than a power rating without that. My guess is the generators on this level are powering items at higher levels above us. Knocking them out could be very useful, disabling additional base defenses and allowing Arctilius to punch through that much sooner, I suppose."**

**"Z9 also seems to be a Geothermal Power Source generator, if we want to shut down their power we'd have to go there, but it seems to be busy at the moment, some kind of firefight from what I overheard."**

"**Hmm.. any indication in how the fight is going? It could be beneficial to lend our support and take out the plant.**" Her finger traces over their possible route, one leading them into another possible confrontation with a Reaper.
**"Not well based on the number of casualties they keep reporting about... and constantly summoning reinforcements to Z9."**
Lexi frowns at the map. "**Did you see the Reaper when you were moving through Z17.. or could I cut through to Z8?**"
**"Definitely some kind of Reaper occupying Z17, sitting at a computer console. Pretty sure I could cut through but... everyone else is going to have to fight through."**
"**If I can get eyes on him then I can keep him preoccupied long enough for the others to get through. My concern is if he sounds an alarm from the console he's at.**"
**"He seems to be talking to somebody, or himself, I can't tell."**
**"If we craft a makeshift exit at Z23 we can bypass him altogether though."**
"**True... It's less direct and we maybe able to sabotage the generator on the way.  The problem becomes the reinforcements.  What route are they taking to Z9.**"
**"They're arriving from the Z7 turbolift and marching south, from what I gathered."**
**"Also Z8 is their staging area, heard em get chewed out real good by the Reaper there, or whomever was in charge."**
"**It's either come in from the north from the Z4 access and cut off the reinforcements or join forces with the squad from this back way.**"
Lexi considers for a long moment and then puts the map away having made her decision. "**We'll reinforce from Z23... Take me to where I'll need to cut us through.**"
Ghost shows Lexi the area of the drainage pipe to slice, and she is able to make an exit for the squad to enter Z23.

A group of 12 black/yellow pattern Stormtroopers were marching through the area as the White Doves make their egress, but thanks to quick reflexes the enemy squad is easily handled and the Commandos find themselves in Z23.
Thankfully, the blaring alarms and the sounds of blaster rifle fire from area Z9 help to cover the sounds of the firefight and it is over within seconds.
Conveniently, stairs lead upwards from the Pipe and onto the base proper. This is the first time the White Doves have entered the base itself and now find themselves on the ground floor.
Z23 seems to be nothing more than a maze of machinery pipes, wires, and other ductwork. In between the guts of the base, there is a series of corridors that lead throughout the level.
It holds doorways leading to Z15, Z21, and Z22.
All are currently locked, but the slain Stormtrooper Commander does hold on his person a Yellow Keycard, when waived over the door panel seems to open it.

Lexi reinforces the squad reflecting shots as they come in range, but true to the promise, they're the best at what they do and soon enough the enemy troopers are dispatched. Presented with the yellow card she's relieved to see that it opens up the doors on the level. "**Well done.. we'll proceed to Z21** As they walk, she glances at the ductwork wondering if someone her size could crawl through in a pinch.
Z21 is similar in construction to Z23, except there is also a supercomputer. A protocol droid is passing through, seemingly paying no mind to the intruders.
Lexi watches the droid for a moment and then motions NZ towards the computer. "**See what you can access.**"
**"Access Denied, apologies master."**

As Lexi uses the Yellow Keycard to enter Z20, she sees a massive Power Generator glowing green energy in the center of the room, with catwalks to and from the Power Generator. As Lexi's squad is atop one of the catwalks, three metal spheres roll out from 3 different directions and unfold into Destroyer Droids, immediately opening fire on the White Doves.
https://www.sideshow.com/storage/product-images/913520/droideka_star-wars_square.jpg

Skinker, Grave, and Leaper take dire hits on their centers of mass and are knocked back. Lexi uses her sabers to block two blaster bolts, redirecting one back at its source. The rest of the troopers open with a withering hail of fire from various weapons platforms including shoulder mounted missile from Jumbo, destroying all 3 Droidekas in a massive display of overkill. Nevertheless, the damage has been done.

Lexi deactivates her lightsabers, her lips set in a grim line as she turns back to the troopers and accesses injuries. "**Let's take a moment and see to our wounded,**" she says, stepping up next to Grave who looks to have sustained the worst from the attack from the droidekas.
**"I'll be fine commander, took a blaster bolt to the shoulder but it cauterized itself. Hurts like a lightsaber but I won't bleed out."**

**"I managed to jump out of the way at the last second."** Leaper lands, as hit jet packs extinguish. **"Second time that trick worked, but honestly I wanted to save my jet fuel for more than just running away..."**
**"Come quick Commander, Skinker got hit in the guts and he's already reading his pet lizards his last will and testatment..."**
**"Alas poor Yorick..."**
Skinker is talking to one of the Ysalamiri and gripping it tightly in front of his face. The lizard seems visible uncomfortable, as the Trooper spills out some slurred words of poetry.
**"Tell Achilles and Hector that they were always... my favorite..."***
**"Hey can I have your stereo system if you don't make it Skinker?"**
Lexi diverts towards Skinker, pulling out her medpac and looks him over. "**Take it easy, Skinker. Let me look you over before you confess your sins to the galaxy.**"

**"Oh thank the Force, an angel. I'm going to heaven boys I always said I'd visit there before you losers..."** Unfortunately his intestines are spilling out thanks to a large hole that basically destroyed his entire digestive system.

Lexi looks through her own supplies and finds it depleted of what she needs. She looks up Bishop hovering nearby, "**Did you pack additional medpacs? I can stabalize him but he'll need painkillers before we can move him.**"
Nodding, Bishop presents a standardized medpac from his belongings and hands them over.
It's got a suicide pill but no painkillers.
It's also got bandages about the quality of toilet paper.
With the medpac in hand she opens it up to at least do what she can to stabalize Skinker so he doesn't bleed out.
Lexi somehow succeeds in packing his guts back in.

As Lexi is doing this, she can for the first time sense the Latent Power within the Purple Seed she received during her Spirit Quest. Additionally,
She is now able to see through a second Purple Verdant Sight, Life Sight, which makes visible in faint purple outlines objects that contain Purple Verdant Energy. Looking down at her own chest, she can see the outline of something growing in her right chest cavity that has a hazy purple outline like a second circulatory system. It's aura is warm and benevolent.

Looking over her handywork, Lexi realizes there was no way they were going to be able to move Skinker in his current condition and she isn't prepared to leave him behind. As she works, she feels the pull of the potential of healing from the purple seeds Okhama had given her in the vision quest. For a moment her own sight seems to blur to be overlaid by a new aspect she's never experienced before. The seeds glow a bright purple and from the wound where she's seen the sprout, purple tendril like roots spread out from the spot. It was something she would have to explore later.

She blinks and pulls out one of the delicate purple seeds and turns her focus back to Skinker. There were consequences to using the Verdant Force she was coming to find out; a price to every bit of extraordinary work. With no time to consult Gellendor on such intricacies, she closes her hand around the seed. 

“**Hold still, Skinker,**” she says softly, before reaching out her other hand and pressing it gently against his belly. Taking a deep breath she lets the Verdant Force flow through her. A green, warm glow flows down her arm to escape where her hand touches him and her eyes suddenly take on the same green tint till she blinks and they return to their usual cyan, glowing brightly for a moment before diminishing.

Drained from the effort, she takes in a shuddering breath and lets it out in a rush. Her shoulders slumping forward slightly from the effort. With the task done, she looks over her handiwork.
**"C-commander...?"** He opens his eyes and blinks, looking down and hardly believing it but his wound has been healed.
**"Hey Sarge, look its a miracle."**
**"It's the Force, gotta be."**
**"Well done Commander, but I have a feeling we better not stick around, someone is going to come wondering what this battle was about..."**
**"The Droidekas have already informed the Command Center about their destruction, expect reinforcements."**

"**That's unfortunate.**" Lexi finds her feet, unsteady at first from the effort the healing she'd just performed. She nods to Bishop as she packs away the remains of the abysmal medpack and gives it back. "**Let's not dally here.**" She heads towards the door for Z11.
Z11 is a charnel house. There are mangled Stormtrooper bodies hanging at odd angles everywhere, having multiple puncture wounds. The main thoroughfare is a long red carpet of blood streaks.
A Supercomputer stands in one corner, drenched in blood.
Some storm trooper limbs lay around strewn like debris, cauterized by lightsabers.
**"Dammit, place gives me the creeps."**
**"What do you suppose did this..."**
A Stormtrooper commander, with his lower torso missing, seem to be conscious somehow, muttering something under his breath.
**"~Cough-They came from... the darkness...~Cough~"** and he vomits blood.
** "Uh... hang on son, you'll be alright..."**
**Yeah uh... help's on the way."**
Whispers: **"Let's *go*."**

Lexi goes over the struggling trooper and looks at his wounds.  It was remarkable he was still living. "**What came?**" she asks, a pit in her stomach growing as she looks about the room at the carnage.  This wasn't the work of a squad of troopers, but of beasts.
**"Ghastly terrors, all teeth and spikes... Please, I can't feel my legs..."**
**"Broke out of Z9, lead by some kind of Super Reaper, cut through a half dozen squads like they were nothing... And killed both our Reapers to boot."**
Lexi visibly goes pale as her suspicion is confirmed and the fact they had just missed him coming out of Z9 seems a blessing.  "**Which way did they go?**" She asks, trying to get what she can.
**"~Glurtch~"** He spits up more blood and goes unconscious.

Lexi pauses a moment to consider their options. Dark Wraith with a host of chimeras were now sweeping their way through the base, having just missed Lexi and her squad in the process. They were likely the ones to have breached through Z9 which meant the Tantis forces would likely be moving to secure it now that they were gone. “**Change of plans…**” she says, turning to look at the door for Z21. She recalls the code names Wraith had assigned for their targets. “**Magnet, what's the chatter like on the channels?**”
The charnel bloodstrains lead to the door for Z12, which appeared barred.
**"Reports of fighting all over the base, Commander."**

Lexi heads over to the door, following the path of the carnage and sets her hand on it as if listening, her senses extending into the room to determine if she could sense Dark Wraith or a Reaper on the other side.

Not picking up anything she steps around the room, looking for any signs of a code cylinder or something that would help them to access the systems more efficiently before moving to the door for Z21. "**We're heading for the upper levels.**"
**"Which way commander?"**
He forms up the squad to breach Z21.
After the door parts, two figures are standing opposite as if expecting Lexi's squad.
**"Greetings intruders. I am Cythera, and this is my counterpart Garathrax. We strongly advise you lay down all of your weapons and surrender, this will be your only polite warning."**
**"Awe, at least give em a chance to fight. I don't know how they managed to kill so many of our brothers and sisters, they don't look very tough..."**

Lexi curses inwardly as the two Reapers stand opposite the door to the room they had cleared shortly before. Outwardly she keeps her stoic calm as she surveys the two Reapers before them. One Reaper she may have handled on her own, but two was likely suicide. "**We didn't... There's a monster on the loose. One created by Xod'mon'orkhez.**" She says, not moving to surrender, but also realizing the current futility at the moment of seeming hostile.
**"Huh? Doctor Xod's back? That's a far fetched story, from what I heard he ran away to lick his test tubes long ago. You really expect us to believe you?"**
"**He's not back.. in fact he's dead. Him and his apprentice Vazz'ri.**" She pauses and gives a small shrug. "**How do you think we found this base? He told us where to find it.**"
The female Sith's head pulses and emits a telepathic ping, **"She's not lying Gar, but she also isn't telling us the whole truth..."**
**"Heheh, that's good. I'll have to tease it out of her twitching corpse then..."**
He unfurls his tunic and drops it on the ground, revealing red tattooed muscles and ignites a saber katana.
**"Who wants to die first?"**

Lexi frowns at the telepath, sensing the intrusion and being unable to stop it. "**He's already easily slaughtered two of your own and several squads has he not? Kill me and you won't learn his weakness.**"
**"Sounds like the mewling weak pleading of a soon-to-be-dead womp rat..."**
**"You are foolish child, there is nothing you cannot keep from my scrying..."**
**"Gar, keep that one alive, for now. The rest you may kill."**
Grave raises his shield and activates his stun baton.
NZ activates his saber staff, twirling and flanking Lexi along the other side.
**"Orders, Commander?"**

Lexi purses her lips as her attempts to delay the fight if not persuade them they were useful alive failed. She keeps herself between the two Reapers and her men as she ignites her two blades. There wasn't much they could do.  Run and be chased down, or stand and fight. "**Take cover,**" she orders evenly to Bishop beside her. "**Be ready to retreat.**" 
Without further comment she charges towards Garathrax, her lightsabers arching in a preemptive attack before he can draw much closer to her men.

Lexi charges heedless of the dangers ahead of her. Sometimes there was no recourse other than to fight head on, if you're outmanned outgunned and outclassed in every sphere of life. Lexi's eyes glow cyan and she couches, springing forward like a hellcat into the flames. The red skinned Zabrak Sith crouches and his eyes flash red as he also bounds forward to meet Lexi's charge. Doing so, he also ignites his second, shorter shotosaber and brings forward both in a cross-shaped gesture to deflect Lexi's double-bladed charge. A furious exchange of blows follows, faster than almost visible to the human eye both melee combatants strike all out furious blows but simply resound against the blades of their opponent, sparks flying in all directions. The Zabrak's expression quivers and falls, as he finds himself backpaddling against Lexi's furious attack. His knee buckles and he's brought down to one knee, as he cannot stand up to the lightning quick onslaught of attacks by Lexi.

The other Sith, the grey-skinned Nautolan who had identified herself as Cytheria, turned both palms upward and began to levitate in the air, green multicolor rainbow psionic energy building up in a floating semi-translucent aura around her cranium. Her robes floating in an otherwordly fashion, the Reaper whips her tail and unleashes a green psychic beam blast from her forehead that scoured both Lexi and her squad. Most members of the White Doves had rolled into cover at Lexi's warning, but one of the squads got caught in the beam - Skinker's Squad. To Cytheria's utter astonishment, the lethal beam of green lambent energy simply dissipated when it impacted on Skinker's Force Void bubble. Cytheria had placed her full force of strength into the Psi Blast, and the impact of its dissipation against the Force Void bubble actually had the exact opposite reaction she had hoped for. Her entire beam of hateful psychic energy had just ran straight into a blank wall. The psychic blowback was so forcefull, that all the energy was reflected straight back at Cytheria. The floating Reaper was smacked full on by an invisible force slam and impacted with a heavy crack against the metal wall in the rear.

Lexi's blades continue their furious attack, the space between the melee combatants becoming a blur of heat and bright saber windmills. Garathrax begins to shout in anger, a long drawn out yell as he feels both pain and extasy, Lexi's disciplined technique dismantling his chaotic feelings-based instinctive style of fighting slowly but surely. At last, the Zabrak was beginning to tire, Lexi's strikes designed to wear down even the most powerful combatant with a sequence of tendon-twisting counterstrikes that would have to be delivered in the exact perfect sequence to avoid being ended instantly. Finally, the Zabrak slipped in his defense just barely, yellow saber biting a long cauterized gash on the Zabrak's exposed skin, adding to his endless cadre of black sith Tattoos. Her green blade had poked a wound along his arm, cauterizing the bleeding but also leaving a nice black scar along his deltoid.

The White Doves knew now was their one chance to attack a Reaper with their guard down, opening up their entire arsenal of weaponry on the floating psychic Reaper. The Reaper, green blood running down her temple along her cranium, simply raised a hand and blocked every single bullet and blaster bolt cold against a psi-shield.

Blaze had gone down to one knee and with a satisfying ~SHHHHICK~ ignites his gout of flame, which bypassed the psychic shield entirely, splashing a gout of flame towards the female Nautoloan, whose skin began to cook and sizzle like eggs on a grill. She gave a satisfying scream of pure pain, as her skin ignited and ran down her body like melted wax. Blaze laughed maniacally:**"Die you fucking freak!"**
The Nautolan was half cooked, but weakly kept herself float.
**"You shall know eternal suffering and pain, Mortals!"**
Lexi's strikes had continued to find their marks, leaving a dozen more gashes across the Zabrak's skin.

With a series of final attacks meant to finish her her opponent, Lexi expertly finished her perfectly executed sequence of attacks, meant to end her opponent's life. At the last second, the Zabrak snarled and instinctively ducked his head at the very last moment, costing one of his horns, which gets neatly severed in place of his neck. Backflipping, breathing heavily, the Zabrak finally gets some distance from the Jedi opponent facing him down. He wipes his mouth, having sustained heavy but noncritical damage along his entire body. Tossing both his lightsabers away from his center mass, they continued floating in a circle around him as he closes his hands into a martial arts hand sign. Casting Sith Sorcery, foul words fell from his lips in the Ancient Sith Language as a torrent of black sand spilled from his mouth and fell on the ground, vomiting forth an entire ton of black sand and partially filling up the room. The sand severely restricted everyone's movement in the area, covering up everyone to their ankles with heavy restricting rock fragments. Somehow, the Zabrak glided along on his toes as if there was nothing but solid ground underneath his feet. Coming on once more, he grabbed both of his sabers from mid air and charged once again at Lexi.

Lexi had been pressing hard on the attack realizing once the Reaper got a foothold he would be able to turn the tables. Such as what happens when he suddenly disengages with her and enacts a strange bit of sorcery to cover them with sand. Her feet now trapped by the thick sediment, she tries to free herself before she finds herself unable to dodge out of his way.

Cytheria had raised her hands above her head and let out a directed subsonic psychic scream, ordinarily in the ocean this would be enough to call any marine wildlife in her hemisphere, here it detonated inside everyone's mind, stunning everyone cold in their place with freezing psychic tendrils that snapped their minds shut. Those unable to resist the psychic scream would find themselves frozen in place, unable to make their limbs move. Fortunately, the Ysalamiri on all the Squad members also made them immune to this attack. Frustrated at this latest failure, the Nautolan created a series of 7 Force Projections of herself and evading the individual lines of fire from the White Doves, hissing in disgust. The squad had answered with a hail of fire, which was directed in the wrong direction but nonetheless ended all the Projections, leaving the psychic Nautolan untouched, but alone once more.

Cytheria disappeared in a ripple of invisible energy covering herself with an Invisibility Shield. Binoc had quickly fumbled with his visor, flipping between heat vision and finding the Nautolan on his scanners, and ignited his red tracker beam to play over the Invisibility Shield, lighting her up as the target. Unfortunately, until he had managed to do so, the Squad was still confused and unable to act. Cytheria had floated into Z13, leaving the combat zone. The White Doves pointed and gave chase to their quarry, impeded by the black sand but making determined efforts to not let the enemy escape.

Back in the center of the room, the Zabrak danced an expert Death Dance amidst the Black Sands, red slashes of lethal saber energy forming a mesmeric show nearly enough to make Lexi lose all her resolve. If not for the valiant example of the White Doves fighting and winning against an opponent that outclassed and outmatched them, Lexi would have likely lose her own resolve. The Zabrak's eyes were glowing so red, as supernatural darkness came to cover the entirety of area Z21. All that was visible was the clashing of sabers. The contrast was so bright that Lexi's mind was nearly overwhelmed. On the other hand, the Zabrak seemed to enjoy pushing them both to their utmost limits of mortality. His instinctive movements gave him the edge in this environment, where both tactile and visual senses were altered and those who relied on Mortal senses alone would surely have been overwhelmed. As it stood, Lexi was taking damage herself, but instinctively curved herself such as to avoid having her vital zones slashed. For the first time since she had donned it, the Robe of the Unknown High Republic Jedi she now bore began to take damage, burning out of the way of the katana saber and shoto saber as Lexi's sleeves sagged and her robes became partly blackened with burn scars.

After his attack sequence had concluded, Lexi found herself covered in burning painful gashes, but the adrenaline pumping through her blood thankfully dulled the pain considerably. The Zabrak had retreated into the Supernatural Darkness. The entirety of Lexi's vision was now blackness. She could still feel the ground beneath her feet, but other senses were all cut off. She would have to summon her Verdant Sight if she were to pierce the Darkness, but having all her concentration on her battle style was making it impossible to focus on Verdant Sight at he same exact time without compromising her guard. The Zabrak's voice echoed in the Darkness, laughing evily. **"Hahahaha... *Experience Fear.* Every Mortal instinctively fears the Darkness, it is a primal instinct no amount of training can surprass. Can you predict where the next strike will land? Will it be your groin? Your neck? Your eye? Will you flinch, Jedi?"**
Having extinguished his sabers, he was now effectively invisible.

Lexi remains eerily calm, her discipline too well honed for the Reaper to illicit the response he desires. She is a Jedi. Confronting fear is the destiny of a Jedi. Her feet shift beneath her as she tries to gauge where the Reaper is.  Unable to sense him within the usual means, she takes a deep breath, opening herself to the flow of the Verdant Force and the Verdant Sight.

Searching into the darkness all she feels is the emptiness and  hears the taunts of her opponent as he tries to disquiet her.
At last she senses him behind her, but reacts too slowly as his blade ignites and slices at her back. She pirouettes, her dual blades in motion as she sweeps them towards the tattooed Zabraki and cleaves him two. As the darkness dispells she sees his final expression: his mouth opens, round with surprise before he drops to the ground. 
She gasps through the pain the adrenaline has been covering and looks around for her squad. 
Remembering seeing them follow the other Reaper into the other room, she charges on to follow them.

In the second room, Lexi rushes in just in time to see her squad overwhelm the cornered telepathic Reaper, her psychic shield cracking and breaking in the air in front of her as blast after blast after missile after slug hits her defenses carving them to nothing. Her last expression is that of a helpless grin, as she meets death head on and her cranium explodes in a fountain of green goo with a single blaster discharge, covering the wall and her trooper alike. Her neck is a smoking stump as the lithe body drops to the ground lifeless.
There is a general cheer in this room as the whole squads lets out a hurrah of victory.
Binoc stands up, re-slinging his sniper rifle over his shoulder as his squad mates pat him on the shoulder pads.
**"Can I stencil a Reaper symbol on my rifle, sarge?"**
He points to a row of stenciled trooper helms on his rifle's underside.

Lexi slows her pace as she sees her squad on the verge of victory and watches them as they celebrate. "**Well done.. you've accomplished something few can boast of.**" She says joining them. She probably looked about how she felt and she tries to keep her expression light with a smile.
The squad turns to her as a whole, noticing her wounds for the first time, **"Are you alright Commander?"**
Each of the bodies hold a Black Keycard. Two sabers are found on the Zabrak. The Nautolan holds a ritual dagger. There are also [2d1000] unmarked Imperial credits between them.

mentions.avatarUrl: Galactic Campaigns - SW_ War of Prophecy - wop_ch5_mount-tantiss [1315812646523637760].json_Files/0b5e281f27b2966df13c6c0150c25eff-a43079ff8ce5b972.png

content:
Lexi waves them off dismissively. "**I've been worse for wear... I'd be even better if I could get a little time to rest.**" She walks over to the body of the headless body of Cythera and pulls out the black key card and her credit chip. She slips the ritual dagger into her belt, intent on looking it over closer later. She hands the credit chip over to Binoc. "**Here you go, trooper... Spoils of war. I'm sure you'll be a good mate and buy the others a drink or two when we get back.**"
Seeing that the Zabraki Reaper also has a key card, she gives this one to NZ before pocketing the credit chip and putting one of his red lightsabers on her belt.
It definitely feels evil slimy and slippery, somewhat distracting Lexi while she holds posession of it (-1 UTF while in her posession).

As her hand comes in contact with the red lightsaber, she feels the unsettling sensation from it and instead tosses his back down on the Reaper's body. The distraction wasn't worth her plan.
She turns back to her squad and looks them over. "**Did she hurt any of you? I saw her psychic attacks rebound off the bubble. I'm glad the ysalmiri were able to protect you from her.**"
**"Negative Commander, she tried to toss some crates at us, but no serious damage was incurred."**
**"The Ysalamiri were the real stars of the show."**
**"You're all welcome by the way.""**
Everyone grumbles but aknowledges Skinker.
This effectively clears Z21 and Z13.

Lexi nods, relieved and gives Skinker a small laugh. "**Yes, indeed.**" She pauses as she looks at the bodies. It wasn't ideal but she needed a little time. "**Let's regroup here... I need to rest and I'm sure you lot could do with one too.**" She looks at Bishop to handle the particulars as she finds a place as far away from the bodies and the Void as she can to heal herself and meditate.

Still feeling the strain after the 15 minutes she had alloted for the rest, only some of her wounds had healed themselves, enough to not have to hide a wince everytime she moved her arms. It was times like these she was envious of Wraith's inherent healing ability. Approaching Bishop she gives him a nod. "**Time to move on...**" She indicates the door heading into the next zone towards the ladder.

This room has a high ceiling, all around the upper half the room a floating squad of Jet Troopers opens up on Lexi's group. They use the many random pipes around this room as cover in their hit and run attacks.

The jet troopers use their cover to great effect, getting off a series of blaster rifle bolts that, although low in power, do substantial damage to the squad, forming them to duck and cover. Lexi tosses a handheld detonator sphere and uses the Force to lift it up towards the nearest concentration of Jet Troopers. Two are disintegrated. Three more fall to return fire from the White Doves, surprisingly accurate despite the harsh fighting environment. Finally, the rest of the Troopers retreat somewhere into the pipes abovehead and the room falls quiet.
**"Recommend we take 5 commander, that would be enough for my troops to apply bacta injections and stim packs, while lingering any longer in this thoroughfare seems ill advised."**

Lexi looks over Grave, Bishop and Skinker realizing they could do with some rest and time to see to their wounds, but their current location just wasn't conducive to the time needed. Instead she addresses her squad. "**Take your stims as needed... we need to push on,**" she orders and waits for them to comply before heading for the door to Z14 and the possible Reaper she had sensed earlier within.
**"Squad apply your stim packs and look alive, we don't have time to take a nap ladies, time is pressing. Get ready to flood the next room."**

Indeed the next room over contains the unmistakable signature of a force user, likely a Reaper, but it seems unusually weak and getting weaker by the second. As Lexi waves the yellow or black keycard over the door control panel, the doors slide open and reveal a singular Reaper that appears to be breathing hard, wounded and dripping purple blood, as it is leaning up against a wall. It's eyes track to see Lexi enter, but it doesn't move to intercept, simply staring and breathing hard, rasping. It is hideous in appearance, and seems to have gotten infected by the Charybdis Virus, growing bone spikes as the nanotechnological Sith Plague is often known to do.

Lexi pauses only a moment as she enters the room and sees the infected Reaper; a sign the Dark One had passed through. Slowly she approaches the Reaper, her lightsaber in hand, but unignited. "**You're infected.... there's no cure,**" she says sadly. "**You'll turn into a beast. If you survive the transformation,**" she comments eying the Reaper's wounds.
It seems to regard you with its infected yellow glowing eyes: **"Heal me, Jedi. I sense you can heal me, if you wished to. Heal me and I will aid you, I promise in the name of the Force."**
**"Don't listen to it, Commander. Let's burn it to a crisp here and now."** A couple of other squad voices murmur in assent.
**"First rule of the battlefield, don't trust your enemy when they ask you for help."**
**"This one reeks of infectant, let's sterilize it like Blaze suggests."**
**"Please spare me, this one no longer wishes to serve the Emperor. This one is called Veq'neviz, a friendly species, transformed through Imperial Science into an unwilling servant of evil."**

Lexi lets the words of her squad wash over her as she watches the desperate Reaper, her senses reaching out get a sense of the Reaper's intent. It was hard for her to turn down someone asking for help. Enemy or no.
Its intent is alien and inscrutable to Lexi.

Lexi steps further toward the Reaper, her expression nuetral as she draws closer. "**You swear on the Force, but even Sith are known to not keep their word.**" She watches the alien carefully. "**I'm here to destroy this place and any Reaper I come across and kill the Emperor to be...**" She grips her lightsaber tightly. "**It would be a mercy for me to kill you now.**"

It coughs sputtering, as purple blood hacks up from its lungs. **"Kill me then if that is your aim, but if you were to heal me, I would pledge eternal service to you, until my life's end.... which I suspect won't be far in any case. Before I leave this world I wish to see the Reapers destroyed, for what they did to me to bring me into existence... I sense your mission here was ordained by the Force itself."**

Pausing only a moment she reaches into the small satchel and withdraws one of the precious purple seeds. "**Swear it then... that you will help me in my task and will see no harm to myself and allies. Swear it by the Force to the Chosen One.**"
**"This one swears it, in the name of the Chosen One of the Force."**

Lexi nods and takes the last step closing the distance between them. She places her hand gently on the alien's exposed skin and focuses, drawing on her connection to the Verdant Force to heal him of the virus coursing through him.

The healing proceeds as best as it can, although the body has been ravaged by the virus for so long that it has been irreversibly damaged, especially near the head wound that was initially inflicted, Lexi is able to use the guiding warm light of the Verdant Force to expunge the infection from the body and halt the transformation. Green beams of light shoot out of the alien's body as the power takes hold, burning away the infection. Afterwards, the alien's skin is looking a lot healthier, although its still been permanently misshapen by its previous condition. The Alien uses its arms to feel itself up and down, stopping on its cranium. **"I feel the infection leaving me, although it seems my body has been permanently and irrevocably altered... Nevertheless... Thank you, Jedi... I would have died had you not happened upon me."** It appears lanky and humanoid, and its voice has been distorted by an unpleasant monstrous shrill bass undertone.
**"This one thanks you, and recognizes you as the Chosen One."**

Lexi withdraws a step as she looks over the irreversibley transformed Reaper. "**Xod'mon'orkhez designed the virus to be highly effective in creating his chimera creatures... It's also a death sentence without the compound used as a failsafe. I'm sure that's why the Dark One has come here, hoping the Storehouse has a stock.**" 
When he recognizes her as the Chosen One she nods in acceptance. "**You know my purpose then?**" It was still unclear to her what knowledge remained of the prophecy, especially with the Jedi extinct.
**"This one only knows what this one has been told, but this one does sense a greater purpose in the aura emanating from the Jedi."**

Lexi gives a small shrug, the knowledge Veq may have regarding the Chosen One was unimportant to the task at hand. "**Did he go that way?**" she asks, motioning towards the door that would lead to the access point to the next level. Changing track she looks back at VZ not forgetting their task. "**We need the code cylinder of someone with security clearance to access the databanks and also the appropriate terminal.**"

**"Negative, after defeating me, this Dark One as you call it, went towards the Control Room. Even now, this one can sense its clawing presence inside. I fear the Jedi will not survive if you fight this Dark One, but perhaps with the aid of this one... the Jedi might stand a small chance."**
Veq'neviz indicates area Z2.
**"It can transform into a titanic monster, none of my brothers or sisters could stand against it, much less the soldiers..."**

Lexi looks back at her squad and at the packs of Ysalamiri they carry. "**His healing ability is profound. We need to negate it if we're to succeed.**" She turns fully to Magnet. "**I need you to send a message through one of the channels...**" With a frown she recalls the codenames Wraith had come up with. "**Put out that Salacious Crumb has been located.**"
Magnet at first hesitates, then nods, and sets his comms pack down to fiddle with the dials.
**"Commander, we received a reply to your message: *Do not eat Salacious Crumb yet, we can sit down and eat together, if we can link up.* "**

Lexi let's out a small exhasperated sigh. The linking up was going to be easier said than done at this point. She looks over at Veq'neviz and Magnet. "**Has there been any communications to indicate where the other teams breached?**"
**"Even if there were, this is such a large compound, they could be anywhere by now, same as us."**
**"If we had computer access, we could obtain a map..."**
**"I may be able to help with that, I don't have a copy of map, that is top secret, but I know roughly the layout of the base."**
**"There are other Jedi here with you...?"**

"**Not Jedi,**" she says not elaborating further. She still wasn't sure how much information she could safely reveal to this enemy turned ally. "**What part do you think you'll be able to help with?**" She asks, looking for clarity.

**"If you need a guide for this base, I can navigate you wherever you need to go. The mountain is linked together by a Light Rail system. This is the Pump Station Complex and Water Filtration and Waste Processing Level. Just tell me where you need to go, and I can help take you there."**
**"Where we are now, the adjacent lower levels include: Main Power Reactor Level, Detention Block Level, Main Computer Core, Barracks, Command Wing and Primary Security Station."**
"**Magnet, send a follow up and request a point to meet up. Mention that the Waste Processing smells worse than Jabba's Palace.**"
**"Commander, if I'm not mistaken the Nighthawks are currently on the Detention Level and are attempting to fight their way forward. They say they could use support."**

**"We could help them break out of their firefight, with joining us we would double our fighting power and could make double the headway going forward, but it would also mean fighting against more stiff resistence... Staying split up into smaller group may also have its merits and allow greater mobility..."**

"**Noted,**" Lexi says as she conisders their options. "**We'll proceed to the detention level and link up with the Nighthawks. Worse case we split up after we aid them in pushing their way through.**"
Her attention turns back to their would be guide. "**Where to?**" she asks. "**We only have a map for this level**"

Veq points to Z1, **"This way to the Light Rail Station, it responds to my Keycard, but... it is monitored on Holocams. If we use it, there's a chance you intruders may be caught by the Security Station personnel, if so Droids will likely be dispatched and the Railcar will be detained until it is cleared by the Droids."**
**"Plus, if there are other passengers aboard that could lead to some awkward commuter conversation..."**

**"Uh... again, I might be able to help with both of those problems, on account of my, unusual Force talents... As an empath this one can project feels of comfort to nearby troops, and make them feel like we belong here."**
"**That would deal with the organics aboard, but still brings up issues with the Holocams... NZ, is it possible for you to hack in and distrupt the feed? Put it on a temporary feed loop?**"
**"I could cut the link and make it look like a malfunction, but to effect a video loop I would still need that Code Cylinder."**
**"I haven't tested my powers across Holocams, but in theory it could work..."**
"**We seem to be dealing with a great deal of conjecture... What about accessing the rail transit line and making our way through that way?**"

**"There is a catwalk above the Light Rail system, I think it might be too low tech to have Holocams along the entire length of it, but surely at key junctions it is very likely to be monitored by Holocam too since it contains the infrastructure for the critical transit system."**
"**How many junctions do you think that will be?**"
**"I couldn't begin to guess, but at least 1 and possibly more."**
"**We'll take our chances with the rail car while our group is smaller. NZ, what do you need to cut the feed?**"
**"I can scramble the Holocam signal if I can get inside of the control panel."**

**"Jedi, I don't think that's such a good idea. If their sensor pick up that were fiddling with the control panel in the railcar, that's a sure way to bring attention to us and call the Security Droids down on our heads."**
**"There's always the plan of just trying to Blend In. You two could act as the Reapers and we just act like we're your subordinates.. and hope the rest goes off without a hitch."**
Lexi nods.  "**We'll keep it in our back pocket. Combined with Veq'neviz's empathy, we can hopefully blend in enough to nor arouse suspicions. For awhile at least.**"

**"What about the Dark One? Do you plan to leave him alone with the Water Treatment Controls? He could surely spread the Virus to every corner of every fresher if he figures out which pipe is the right one..."**
Lexi considers not liking the idea on bit of him spreading the virus. "**Could we sabotage the system without confronting him?**"
**"Killing the Reactor powering this level might do the trick, but only if we also travel to the Main Reactor to shut it off too, otherwise it'll just be used as a backup to power the Pumps here."**
**"And as you can imagine the Main Reactor is heavily, heavily guarded."**

**"I think we're prioritizing targets incorrectly here. Our first target should be the Central Computer Core, if we can compromise that or effect a manual backdoor, the rest of the infiltration would be a cakewalk. Of course, I think the enemy knows it too, and will probably be on guard, especially with the sirens blaring."**
"**If we take control of the computer core, will we be able to turn off the systems here and prevent the virus spread?**"
**"If we take over the central computer, we could make this base do whatever we want."**

With a nod her brain shifts to the new objective. "**Let's proceed then. As a smaller group with Veq'neviz's aid, we may be able to approach unmolested and catch them unawares while they deal with the other squads.**" Her gaze goes back to Veq. "**Any ideas on how we can use the Dark One as a distraction and have more forces sent his way?**"
**"Blowing the Reactor is sure to get their attention."**
**"We wanna be far away when that timer goes off, though."**
"**Do we have the munitions needed to set up a trigger or timed detonation?**" she asks Bishop and the others of the squad.
**"Yes Commander, we packed demolitions as ordered."**
**"Jumbo is carrying them."**
Lexi nods to Bishop and Jumbo acknowledging the information. "**Let's go set up a distraction and then move on to the Core. Will blowing the Reactor here interfere with the Rail?**"
**"Highly unlikely Commander."**
The Squads sets off to follow Lexi's orders.
**"Demolitions set at the ready, Commander, timer has been primed and prepped."**
**"That only leaves me with a dozen functioning warheads for the missile launcher though..."**

"**Well done. I'm sure the sacrifice will be worth it if it keeps the Tantiss forces and the Dark One occupied. We'll blow it once we get closer to the Rail.**" She looks at them to see if there was any better suggestions before looking at Veq. "**Let's get going.**"
**"How many minutes would you like me to set on the timer Commander?"**

Along the way to the Light Rail, the Squad enters Z1 and bypasses the Security Door with either the Black or Yellow Keycard. A long concrete tube with schematics of the Light Rail Stations on one wall is visible. B1, the Water Pumps, connects through a long tube rail to B2, the Detention level. Apparently, the next Railcar is due to arrive in about 8 minutes.
C1, the Computer Core, is directly above the Detention Level.
"**How long is the ride from here to the Core?**" she asks trying to calculate the length the timer would need to be.
**"About 12 minutes, assuming no unusual stoppage of traffic."**

"**Set the timer for 15 minutes, Jumbo.**" As they reach the entrance to the rail car she glances about them, the sight of it a new experience for her. "**By the way... you can call me Lexi. It will be better than 'Jedi' if we're to pull this off with the least amount of suspicion.**"
**"Yes, Jedi Lexi, I agree. I would look suspicious calling you The Chosen One everywhere... please call me Veq."**
**"Roger that ma'am, Timer has been all set."**
**"Uh-- Thir-fif-fifteen minutes ma'am."**
After 8 minutes pass, a new Hover Rail Car arrives at Station B1.
"**Yes, well.. you're one of a few who know this. For obvious reasons I'd like to keep it such.**" Her attention turns to Jumbo. "**13 should be fine Jumbo.**"
The Railcar Doors swoosh open and 12 Death Troopers emerge, followed by an Imperial General in grey uniform.
The General looks over at Lexi's band, who've frozen and are not sure what to do, **"You two, Reapers, give me a status update on this sector."**
The Death Troopers fill in on either side of the commander.
**"Ah, G-General Khird, of course sir."**
**"Your timing couldn't be better, I'll let my fellow Reaper give you the full details."**

Lexi eyes the General and the squad of Death Troopers who have exited the tram with him and blinks in surprise. At the corner of her gaze she looks over at Veq before turning her full attention to the General. Inwardley she sighs as he leaves her to speak to the man. "**We have reason to believe the breachers have moved on from Z9 and has taken up position in the control room of Z2,**" she says, sticking with the what she knew was being sent over the comms.

The General furrows his bushy brows at this news, and proclaims in a crisp commanding imperial accent, patronizing and arrogant, **"Reapers - useless as always. You're telling me you've let the enemy take up residency in the Control Room under your very noses? And who are these Stormtroopers, I don't recognize this Squad at all. Are they recent transfers? Why have you not been issued proper regulation Tantisstrooper Armor? What a bunch of incompetents... but what did I expect coming here to the bowels of the mountain... You rookies will have to suffice. Hold off the enemy as best you can, I will leave my personal Death Troopers to help reinforce this position."** He nods to the Death Troopers, **"Make sure they do their job right this time, I'm taking the Tram back up to Command Level and summoning more reinforcements to your area."**
**"Understood, General."**
"**We were being sent to reinforce our forces on the Detention level, General,**" she says deciding to take a risk in lying.

The General stops and turns around becoming nearly beligerent, **"I don't care what you were sent to do, if you were sent to pick your noses or reapply make up, it makes no difference, when I give you an order you follow it, Reaper. Do not forget you are property, just an object, little more than a droid in the eyes of the Empire. If a droid doesn't follow orders, it will be decommissioned, do I make myself clear, Reaper?"**
Without waiting for a reply, he steps one step back onto the Tram and the doors close, whisking him away.

A tense silence follows as the Death Troopers and White Doves eye each other, everyone picking up instinctively on the fact that the energy and vibes in the room, aren't quite right between the parties involved.
**"You, Stormtrooper Commander. What's your operating number?"**
**"It's uh-- Zed Eleven Kappa Niner. What's yours?"**
**"Trigon Omega Thirteen. I don't recognize your ident code."**
**"You heard the General, we're newly transfered, can't you uh... can't you see just by looking?"**

Inwardly Lexi sighs at the turn their plan had gone. It wouldn't be long now before the Reactor blew and while they had hoped to be on their way to the Computer Core at this point, they now had to contend with the Death Troopers. "**Remedial duties.. Why do you think we got stuck down here with them?**"

**"Friends friends, let's not quarrel amongst ourselves. There's a perfectly good target in the form of an enemy, taken up residence next door. Do you wish to do their job for them and cause a firefight to break out here and now over some silly ident code mixup? Don't we all have larger Mon Cal to fry?"**
The Death Trooper's stance seems to soften, and he grunts, **"Fine, but after the intruders are repelled I plan on bringing up this matter to the MPs officially."**

**"Whatever you want, soldier, whatever you want. Just help us to get rid of the enemy entrenched position. They've heavily fortified Z2 as my colleague said and we need your aid in retaking that position. Why don't we enter from the side using our lightsabers while you try to punch through the door. Does that sound agreeable?"**
**"I think that's just about enough lies out of you... Death Troopers, open fire!"**

At the end of the brief but bloody firefight, the Death Troopers are cut down but the White Doves take many hits and one casualty: Skinker has been shot through the heart, and is currently bleeding out.
Last second, Lexi is able to summon the Verdant healing energies of the Force to regrow his heart with a burst of supernatural healing, Skinker passes out but is still alive.
The squad recovers, getting used to Skinker's near death experience. His extraordinary luck and near misses have earned him some level of notoriety among the squad members.
**"He keeps coming back to life, like the mythical eternal life giving serpent. How fitting for our reptile specialist."**
**"The universe isn't ready to part with this one, it seems."**
**"A pity the Death Troopers didn't buy the story you two were spinning..."**
**"Ah guys I hate to remind you but we're on a timer, the reactor here will blow in like 5-ish minutes..."**

Lexi is pleased she's able to heal Skinker, bringing him from the brink once more, but the repeated healing is beginning to leave its toll on her. She pats Skinker on the shoulder, doing her best to conceal her growing tiredness with a smile. They were all getting tired afterall over the ordeal and they needed her spirits high. With the reminder she looks at when the next tram is set to appear.
Tram will be arriving in 4 minutes.
**"Want me to turn around and diffuse the charges?"**

Lexi considers whether heading along the catwalk might not be the better option at this point. "**Would the reinforcements be coming this way you think or via the turbolift on the other side?**" Looking at Jumbo she shakes her head. "**No... the charges sesrve a two fold purpose. A way to bring more attention to the Dark One, and slow down him putting the virus into the water.**"
**"So far they've been using the turbolift to bring in most of their personel."**

"**It's going to be cutting it close... and we risk another run in,**" she says talking to herself. "**I think we need to risk the catwalk and hope when the charges go off that's enough of a distraction when we hit the intersections.**"

The Squad lines up and through the service entrance door upstairs to the catwalk above the light rail tunnel. As they jog forth, behind them they hear a detonation and drop down on the catwalk, as it shakes and the shockwave tremors make little lines of pebbles stream from the ceiling. The tram had just gone in the direction of the station the squad had vacated, bearing another load of reinforcing stormtroopers. A single holocam watches them arrive at the station and file off running into the complex a minute before the reactor detonates.
Shouts of hooting and cheering are heard, and jumbo gets padded on the back.
**"And that's how you do it."**
"**Well done... Let's get going before we lose any element of surprise.**"
The squad is slowed down significantly, not having the benefit of moving by fast hovertram.
It takes roughly two hours to walk to the Core Computer tram station, where the squad is finally able to exit the narrow confines of the railtram catwalk.
Lexi reaches out with all her senses to determine what lays beyond in the level with the Core Computer and what they might expect.

Lexi is surprised to learn that using her Verdant Sight, she can almost sense the entire layout of the level, thanks to the fact that the Force is a literal energy field that penetrates all things. She intuits that if she were to consume another purple Life Seed, she might be able to use the Force to map the entirety of this level.
Lexi reaches into the pouch of seeds and grasps one of the Verdant energy sources in her hand. She draws on it to extend her Sight to get the improved perspective.

Lexi lets out her breath as the impressions for the level come to her and she opens her eyes to look over at Veq. "**Are you familiar with this level at all?**" she asks, if he had some knowledge to where they should be going, it would save them time rather than moving about with indirect purpose.
**"Never been here before, sorry."**

Lexi nods, only slightly disappointed, but not surprised. She indicates the first door on their left heading into room 4. "**There's someone in there... let's see what we can learn.**" Turning her attention to squad she motions for them to hang back. "**Stay low, we might have better luck without having to explain your presence just yet.**"

Entering the room, Lexi gets the sense that she stepped in on an evaluation in progress. The room is small, about 4 by 3 meters and has no furniture in it. Instead, one of the walls is a one-way transparisteel mirror through which the larger room beyond can be viewed. Through the transparisteel, three Reapers can be seen sparring with each other, each displaying rather gruesome mutations and grotesque deformities, which they are using to great effect in the three-way melee. Watching the battle, in the same room as Lexi, stands a trio of Imperial Scientists, intent on the battle before them. As Lexi walks in, they all turn to look at her questioningly.
One of the scientists, a strikingly handsome redhead, turns and asks Lexi in an arrogant Imperial accent, **"Are you lost little girl?"**

Lexi blinks looking between the trio of scientists and the melee being observed in the room beyond. Ignoring the condescending tone she lets her natural confusion come to the fore. "**Apologies for interrupting. We must have taken a wrong turn...**" She turns to Veq her brows narrowing as if displeased. "**I told you it was the last junction.**"
Two of the scientists turn back to watch the melee, already losing interest. However, the red head continues to observe Lexi, her gaze inscrutible.
**"Just a moment, what did you say your name was... You don't look familiar..."**
"**I didn't,**" Lexi drawls, and turns to step out of the room and away from the scientist's suspicious gaze.
As there just so happens to be a major clash going on in the next room, something in the action beyond catches the scientist's attention and she finally turns away from Lexi.
**"This room is reserved, you may take your leave..."**
Not needing to be told twice, Lexi lets the door close behind her. She frowns as she looks down the hallway and considers her mental map.
**"This one recognized those people... Reaper scientists..."**
"**I gathered as much by how intently they were studying the melee...**"
"**Do you know if this level has labratories for the program in addition to the Core?**"
